races.race_name: CAA Stellar Handicap

runners.name: Canon's House
runners.spotlight: Did well last term, winning four times; back in top form, beaten only narrowly, at Beverley (5f, good to soft) on Monday with reappearance under his belt; may remain competitive off same mark, provided he takes well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Went close at Beverley on Monday; may remain competitive, provided he takes to headgear
runners.owner: M McHale, J Munroe, M Guy & D Walshe

runners.name: Roach Power
runners.spotlight: Posted duck eggs over 6f in last two appearances, not beaten entirely by the trip; has a very good record at 5f but faces a harder task off current mark at this level, especially from an unfavourable draw.
runners.comment: Has a very good record at 5f but faces a harder task off current mark in this field

runners.name: Stratusnine
runners.spotlight: Similar type to stablemate Dubai Bling, having raced mostly over 6f; beat him readily on similar terms at Wolverhampton in December and ran well at Newcastle in latest AW start; has a record of 2212 on turf and may do better still; interesting, assuming he copes with the drop in distance.
runners.comment: Ran well in latest AW start; record of 2212 on turf and may do better still; interesting

runners.name: Smart Vision
runners.spotlight: Good spell culminated in an October double; form has dipped sharply since and he is due a 2lb drop in future; close second in sole C&D attempt but this is harder and doesn't look as if he's about to add to his tally.
runners.comment: Good spell culminated in October double; form has dipped sharply since
runners.owner: J E Abbey And New Vision Bloodstock

runners.name: Dubai Bling
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 6f, most recently winning big-field handicap (good to firm) for a bright end to his spell in Bahrain; this local runner is well drawn returned to British soil and has possibilities provided he's as effective back at 5f.
runners.comment: Won his final start of a spell in Bahrain; well drawn returned to British soil; in the mix

runners.name: Ruby's Profit
runners.spotlight: Has gained all wins in spring, the most recent off current mark at Goodwood (5f, good) last May; finished nicely for close fourth at Bath (wore hood) on Monday with reappearance under her belt; fighting chance from a handy draw.
runners.comment: Has gained all wins in spring; finished nicely at Bath on Monday; fighting chance

runners.name: Seven Questions
runners.spotlight: Disappointing favourite at Ripon last time, taking record for current yard to 0-10, but his reappearance was encouraging and he has become very attractively handicapped; peak mark was 18lb higher and earned by bagging Group 3 honours at Newmarket two years ago; has the plum draw and can't be written off.
runners.comment: 0-10 for current yard but he's attractively treated on best form and has the plum draw

runners.name: Atomic Force
runners.spotlight: Successful in last two AW attempts but wasn't in anything like the same form returned to turf last time (effective in this sphere in the past); remains on career-high mark and can be opposed kept to grass.
runners.comment: Successful in last two AW attempts; form dipped back on turf last time; opposed

runners.name: Roman Dragon
runners.spotlight: Chester specialist whose seven course wins include this race last year (off current mark under Oisin Murphy) and Listed event later in 2025; inconsistent elsewhere since but commands respect returned to the Roodee; favourably drawn.
runners.comment: Seven course wins include this race last year off current mark under Oisin Murphy

runners.name: Dapper Valley
runners.spotlight: Registered a 1l win at Salisbury (5f, good to firm) on Sunday, taking record since rejoining James Owen to 2-2 and overall strike-rate for this stable to 5-7; those stats make him impossible to dismiss; incurs 5lb penalty in a stronger race but Christian Howarth takes off a handy 3lb.
runners.comment: Record of 5-7 in his races for James Owen, most recently winning on Sunday; not ruled out

runners.name: Vintage Clarets
runners.spotlight: Close third off 6lb higher (from stall 5) in this contest two years ago and 2-4 at Chester overall; however, has a big doubt over current form, reflected in two duck eggs this season; wide draw adds uncertainty.
runners.comment: Close third in this race two years ago but has a big doubt over current form

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Caturra Lights
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a 6f winner; belied his 66-1 odds with close third of eight, faring best of the newcomers, in Musselburgh maiden (5f, good); sets the standard, assuming he can back up that form.
runners.comment: Sets the standard, assuming the form of his 66-1 third at Musselburgh is backed up

runners.name: Jazz Queen
runners.spotlight: Related to a Nunthorpe winner; perhaps hindered by ending up rather isolated towards the near side in maiden (5f, good) at Newmarket, finishing sixth of eight; this local runner is likely to do better, provided she can overcome wide draw.
runners.comment: Perhaps hindered ending up rather isolated in Newmarket maiden; likely to do better

runners.name: Koodini
runners.spotlight: March foal; 20,000euros yearling; sibling to two winners, notably Old Chums (6f; RPR 84); dam 6f/1m scorer (including 2yo/Listed; 103), half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Piece Of Paradise; suitably bred, nicely drawn and represents a locally based trainer who landed this race with a debutant last year; interesting.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling; suitably bred, nicely drawn and looks an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Donegal Rose
runners.spotlight: Closely related to a 7f AW 2yo winner; the form of her fifth of 12 at Leicester (5f, good to firm) needs marking up a tad as she was hampered in the closing stages; local runner who has Oisin Murphy on board and warrants respect with improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: Form of her Leicester effort needs marking up as she was hampered in the closing stages

runners.name: Andalnade
runners.spotlight: April foal; 52,000euros foal, 37,000gns yearling; Showcasing half-sister to five winners, most notably Sacramento (Australian 9.5f/1m2f; RPR 97); dam 7f AW 2yo scorer (88); wears tongue-tie on debut; has the plum draw.
runners.comment: 37,000gns yearling; by Showcasing; wears tongue-tie on debut; has the plum draw

runners.name: Penny Capri
runners.spotlight: January foal; 32,000euros foal, 42,000gns yearling, £50,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Minzaal and first foal of 6.5f-1m winner (RPR 94); fitting of hood on debut suggests she's possibly fizzy; market may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: £50,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Minzaal; wears hood on debut; check the betting

runners.name: Paper View
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to seven winners; unable to justify favouritism but showed some promise with fourth of ten at Bath (5f, good) and ought to progress; possibilities, provided he's able to overcome the widest draw.
runners.comment: May build on Bath promise, provided he's able to overcome the widest draw

runners.name: Dubai Champion
runners.spotlight: March foal; 38,000euros foal, 40,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Persian Force half-brother to two winners, notably Mansa Musa (5.5f/6f including 2yo/US Listed; RPR 113); dam 6f AW scorer (74); well-drawn debutant.
runners.comment: 40,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Persian Force; well-drawn debutant
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Sunrise At Dawn
runners.spotlight: Made encouraging late gains on her way to fifth of 11 at Beverley (5f, good to soft) and may improve with the experience under her belt, albeit is perhaps going to need a strong pace if again closing from rear.
runners.comment: Made late gains at Beverley but may need a strong pace if again closing from rear

runners.name: Undercover Affair
runners.spotlight: Speedily bred gelding who finished about 2l fourth of seven in Bath maiden (5.7f, firm) won by a 40-1 stablemate, shaping as if this sharper scenario will suit; in the mix, provided he can surmount wide draw.
runners.comment: Speedily bred; Bath effort suggests this sharper scenario will suit; in the mix

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced at Beverley (5f) on Monday but it's questionable whether he improved on his effort behind Caturra Lights at Musselburgh; vulnerable sort, taking on once-raced rivals and newcomers.
runners.comment: Questionable whether he improved on Monday; vulnerable in this field

races.race_name: Boodles Raindance Dee Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Morshdi
runners.spotlight: Dante and Derby entry; brother to 1m2f and 14.5f (Group 2) winner Nakheel (RPR 110); won at York (1m, good) on his second start as 2yo and followed up in clearcut style in a seven-runner Listed race at Newmarket (1m1f, good) three weeks ago; has one of the two best chances on form and he's on his way up.
runners.comment: Won second 2yo start and in clearcut style in 1m1f Listed race at Newmarket last month

runners.name: Constitution River
runners.spotlight: Group 1 entry at 1m, 1m2f and 1m4f; by Wootton Bassett out of unplaced sister to 1m4f/1m6f Group 1 winner Wonderful Tonight; short-headed at Newmarket before making all when odds-in in both a Galway maiden (7f, yielding) last July and five-runner Group 2 at the Curragh (7f, good) last August; both wins came with authority and the pedigree is encouraging for 1m2f; his enthusiasm for getting on with it last year raises more of a question but the 1m2f French Derby has been mentioned by his trainer as an objective.
runners.comment: Enthusiasm for getting on with it and twice made all over 7f as 2yo, notably in a Group 2
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Generic
runners.spotlight: By Kameko out of 1m2f winner (77) with Group performers in family; led over 1f out when pipped in a Kempton maiden (1m, AW) in March but a win never seemed in doubt when he made virtually all in Yarmouth novice (1m, good) 17 days later; looks promising for 1m2f but this race is much more demanding.
runners.comment: Looks promising for 1m2f but this is much more demanding than the Yarmouth novice he won

runners.name: Flushing Meadows
runners.spotlight: Group 1 entry at 1m, 1m2f and 1m4f, as is his stablemate Constitution River (also by Wootton Bassett); can't match his form, having won on debut but come up short in three Group 3s over 6.5f to just over 7f; joint favourite at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance but slowly away and never showed with any chance; it's possible that 1m2f will see him in a different light but Constitution River looks the stable first string.
runners.comment: 1m2f might see him in a different light but Constitution River looks the yard first string
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Shayem
runners.spotlight: Dante entry; progressive with 3-4 record in Britain last season, ending with a Listed win from the front at Pontefract (1m, soft); however, beaten a long way in Saudi Arabia this February and although back near his 2yo form in Listed race at Newmarket (1m1f, good; disputed lead 2f out) three weeks ago, that was good enough for only fifth of seven to Morshdi; major work to do.
runners.comment: 3-4 record in Britain last season; only fifth of seven to Morshdi in a recent Listed race

runners.name: Golden Story
runners.spotlight: Dante and Derby entry; by Lope De Vega out of well-bred mare whose three earlier offspring have best RPRs of 112, 103 and 112, including this one's brother the 2025 Great Voltigeur third Arabian Force; 13-8 favourite when winning a five-runner maiden at Doncaster (1m, soft) last October, leading 2f out and looking set for a wide-margin win before rather pulling himself up in the last half furlong; probably best forgiven that indiscretion first time out, in which case he's a good prospect for 1m2f+.
runners.comment: Rather pulled himself up when winning at Doncaster (1m, soft); otherwise a good prospect

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Ormonde Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Jan Brueghel
runners.spotlight: Underperformed in the King George on last appearance, having set a pace that perhaps wasn't strong enough given his proven stamina (St Leger winner); cheekpieces worn that day are now removed; mostly progressive otherwise, still has a good strike-rate (5-7) and showed tip-top form to beat Calandagan in the 2025 Coronation Cup at Epsom; the pick on ratings, accordingly.
runners.comment: Record is 5-7; showed tip-top form to win the 2025 Coronation Cup; the pick on ratings
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Mount Atlas
runners.spotlight: Close second in Listed events at Chester (12.5f) last September and Musselburgh (1m6f) on reappearance, latter behind Al Qareem who gave 5lb and is now better off; further behind that opponent when fifth of six in this race last year; needs a personal best.
runners.comment: Ran well on reappearance but this is harder; fifth of six in this contest last year

runners.name: Rahiebb
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive in his first season last year, despite being 0-6 after debut success; neck second to market leader and emerging star stayer Scandinavia in the St Leger (14.5f, soft) final start; threatening to land a major prize and, the way his figures are going, this 4yo season could prove fruitful; an interesting challenger to the two Ballydoyle runners in this field.
runners.comment: Neck second in the St Leger; looks an interesting challenger to the Ballydoyle runners

runners.name: Beylerbeyi
runners.spotlight: Versatile and consistent handicapper who landed the 2025 Cesarewitch (2m2f) in most recent turf attempt and has been placed in all AW starts (1m2f-2m) this year; however, faces a very difficult task at the weights upped in class.
runners.comment: Versatile and consistent handicapper but faces a difficult task upped in class

runners.name: Sons And Lovers
runners.spotlight: Very useful for current yard; has been on his travels this year, winning 1m7f handicap at Riyadh then creditable fourth over 2m at Meydan, both Group 2; however, incurs 5lb penalty and faces a stiffer assignment conceding weight all round in this field.
runners.comment: Incurs 5lb penalty for Group 2 handicap win at Riyadh; faces a stiffer assignment

runners.name: Illinois
runners.spotlight: Ran as if something was amiss in the Irish St Leger on last appearance; very consistent otherwise and justified favouritism in this contest (good) 12 months ago, prior to second-place finishes in two Cup races; beaten only a neck by stablemate Jan Brueghel in the 2024 St Leger; respected in rematch with that rival.
runners.comment: Beaten only a neck by stablemate Jan Brueghel in 2024 St Leger; won this race last year
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Al Qareem
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism in Listed grade at Musselburgh (1m6f, soft) on seasonal debut, making it five wins from his last nine starts and taking overall record to 13-36; Chester record reads 112, most recently running creditably in this race 12 months ago; again likely to give his running.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate; Chester record reads 112, the final effort in this race 12 months ago

runners.name: Real Dream
runners.spotlight: Best effort this year, but still below peak form, when close fourth to Sons And Lovers in Group 2 handicap at Riyadh; failed to beat a rival on AW last time, hasn't won since 2023 and faces a much stiffer assignment on these terms.
runners.comment: Hasn't won since 2023 and faces a stiff task on these terms

races.race_name: Xenon Workplace Handicap

runners.name: Factual
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt whose solid novice record comprises a good effort flanked by turf/AW wins, latest over 7f at Newcastle 11 weeks ago when showing a game attitude by rallying; looks the type who could progress and do well in handicaps; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Has a solid novice record; looks the type who could progress and do well in handicaps

runners.name: Strength Of Spirit
runners.spotlight: Winner of Chester seller (7f) last June and has shown progress to add two handicaps elsewhere (overall record is 3-6); good third at Haydock (1m, good to firm) most recently; holds solid claims and should go well.
runners.comment: Record of 3-6 includes a Chester win; good effort last time; solid and should go well
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Cool Molly
runners.spotlight: Progressive on AW; has a record of 4-6 this year and 2-2 since upped to 7f, most recently scoring at Southwell; the question is whether she can transfer the excellent work to this new scenario (turf debut).
runners.comment: Progressive on AW; record of 4-6 this year; switch to turf is the question mark

runners.name: Utmost Good Faith
runners.spotlight: Turf winner (claimer) in Ireland but has shown better RPRs on AW for new stable; failed to transfer the form back to this sphere latest start, finishing last of six at Musselburgh; others preferred.
runners.comment: Failed to transfer his AW form back to turf last time; others preferred

runners.name: Moonfall
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since finishing last of seven in Newmarket Group 2 last July; solid novice form otherwise, culminating in Newbury success from the front (6.5f, good); may still have more to offer and looks interesting dropped back in class.
runners.comment: Solid novice form prior to Group 2 defeat; may still have more to offer; interesting
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Archer Royal
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant whose chance off top weight partly depends on whether he can reproduce peak effort, namely third in 7f Group 3 at Newmarket last September, which was the second of his four outings.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant whose chance depends on whether he can reproduce peak effort (Group 3)

runners.name: McMurray
runners.spotlight: Has a record of 3-5 overall and 2-2 since handicapping, most recently beating sole rival at Yarmouth (1m, good) seven months ago; beat a total of 16 opponents in gaining his other wins; major contender, with further improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Has a record of 3-5 overall and 2-2 since handicapping; further improvement is plausible
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: I'm Just Ken
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding whose novice form suggests he could do well in handicaps at the right level; however, this is a tough starting point and he's the only maiden in the field; yard also runs King Of Thunder.
runners.comment: Could do well in handicaps at the right level but this is a tough starting point

runners.name: Command The Stars
runners.spotlight: Has seemed a non-stayer over 1m2f and 1m1f (did too much up front) in last two appearances; steadily progressive form at 7f-1m otherwise, culminating in French Listed win; hood now fitted; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Drops back from 1m1f; has steadily progressive form in his 7f-1m races; not ruled out
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Monarch's Gold
runners.spotlight: Campaigned only at 6f, mostly on AW (admittedly went close in sole turf attempt); soundly beaten on finals day at Newcastle most recently; 0-3 since handicapping and now goes into unknown territory trip-wise.
runners.comment: Campaigned only over 6f, mostly on AW; goes into unknown territory trip-wise

runners.name: King Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Local runner who is 1-1 at handicap level and may well show further progress; gelded prior to that Haydock success (7f, good to soft; held on narrowly) in October; should be well primed for this reappearance assignment, given the location, and warrants major respect.
runners.comment: Local runner bidding to make it 2-2 in handicaps; should be well primed; highly respected

runners.name: Caballo Grande
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-2 on AW; doesn't look particularly solid on his two turf efforts, latest at Newbury (handicap debut) where he ended up last of 14 having raced freely and been eased once dropping away.
runners.comment: Record of 2-2 on AW; ended up last of 14 at Newbury returned to turf latest start

runners.name: Aqua Bear
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish runner who has been absent and gelded since justifying favouritism in Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) in November on stable debut; needs progress to follow up but market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Gelded since winning AW maiden in November on stable debut; needs progress to follow up

runners.name: Gentle George
runners.spotlight: Showed progressive RPRs over 5f/6f for Mick Appleby, winning twice prior to an apparently good effort on Champions Day (mark raised 6lb for that); sold for 90,000gns since; something to prove upped in trip.
runners.comment: Showed his 2yo form for Mick Appleby over 5f/6f; something to prove upped in trip

races.race_name: Halliwell Jones Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Jupiter Ammon
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over about 1m2f on four of eight starts last year, including in two handicaps and one was at this track; disappointing when 4-7 in a novice event on reappearance but back-to-form fourth of 14 in 1m2f handicap at Newbury on latest start; place claims again and cheekpieces are added.
runners.comment: Often bang there but he's 0-10; place claims again and cheekpieces are added

runners.name: Seagolazo
runners.spotlight: Sole win was a maiden as 2yo on his only run at this track; none too consistent in his 11 starts since but recent reappearance was respectable and this return to 1m2f should be okay; record with blinkers or cheekpieces (both absent last time) gives encouragement for today's first-time visor; wide draw.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but headgear record gives encouragement for this first-time visor; wide draw
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Rastnet
runners.spotlight: 9.4f novice winner who's been runner-up over 1m4f/1m3f in all three of her handicaps; return to 1m2f may well be okay and she'll have a shout of the form is transferred on this turf debut.
runners.comment: 9.4f novice winner; runner-up over 1m4f/1m3f in all three of her handicaps; turf debut

runners.name: Organ
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick over 7f/1m on good/good to firm in May and June last year; right out of form in August/September but reappearance fourth at Epsom (8.5f) nine days ago was a step back in the right direction; looks bred for 1m2f (has had one attempt) but may need a career best.
runners.comment: Reappearance fourth at Epsom (8.5f) was step back in right direction; looks bred for 1m2f

runners.name: Londoner
runners.spotlight: Usually thereabouts, including in AW campaign this year which culminated in a Lingfield 1m2f win five weeks ago; one concern is that he was below form last September on his only run at this track.
runners.comment: Usually thereabouts but he was below form last September on his only run at this track

runners.name: Harvey
runners.spotlight: Turf winner in France as a juvenile but ran just twice last year; career is advancing again now for new yard, with one AW third followed by his short-head win at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft; came from towards rear) in March, so he needs a close look despite the absence of this season's hood.
runners.comment: Short-head Doncaster win in March; this season's hood is absent, but needs a close look
runners.owner: Miss E Nolan And Paul Hancock

runners.name: Spioradalta
runners.spotlight: Five wins last year, one of them over C&D, and he's back on the scoresheet already after his narrow win at Ripon three weeks ago; up 5lb and drawn widest, but the first two were clear last time and he's never been better.
runners.comment: Five wins in 2025, one of them C&D, and he's back on the scoresheet in 2026; drawn widest

runners.name: Dante's Lad
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m2f winner on a sound surface early last summer but inconsistent since; first of four runs for new yard at Meydan this year was easily the best; drawn wide but has a good mark if back to form.
runners.comment: First of four runs for new yard at Meydan was easily the best; drawn wide; good mark

runners.name: Galyx
runners.spotlight: Peak efforts have been on soft or heavy ground, including two wins as 3yo and a creditable place last June when last seen; only eight races in total and he could still have more to give, but substantial rain may be needed.
runners.comment: Peak efforts on soft or heavy ground; only eight races in total, last seen last June

runners.name: Celeborn
runners.spotlight: Solid as 2yo (three races, won debut) and clearcut winner in 13-runner handicap at Epsom (8.5f, good) last April; 9lb higher today but looked set for further progress; he has a mixed pedigree for stamina (by middle-distance sire out of 5f winner) and is drawn wide but has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Off since winning a 13-runner handicap at Epsom (8.5f, good) last April; 9lb higher today
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Wanderlust
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of his first six starts in France before comfortably making all in a maiden at Angers (10.5f, soft) last September; left Godolphin and Andre Fabre for 44,000gns in October; gelded since; has wide draw for this British debut, probably needing some improvement.
runners.comment: Made all in maiden at Angers (10.5f, soft) last September; left Andre Fabre for 44,000gns

runners.name: Let's Dream
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (made most) on reappearance at this meeting last year and, from four return visits, he placed twice last July; ended the campaign with a career best to win at York (1m2f, good; came from midfield) in October, which sees him raised 6lb today but he has to be in serious calculations.
runners.comment: C&D winner (made most) on return at this meeting last year; ended 2025 on a high at York

runners.name: Bravais
runners.spotlight: Smart form at his best in France and Ireland; however, latest start was the first time he's beaten a rival in his four runs for new yard, and even then he beat just one; cheekpieces (tried once last year, well beaten) return; at least he's way down the weights.
runners.comment: Behind in his four runs for yard; cheekpieces (tried once) return; way down the weights

runners.name: Hamadhan
runners.spotlight: Ran in four Irish maidens, best when never-nearer third of 14 at Dundalk (10.5f, AW debut) last September on final outing; left Johnny Murtagh and Aga Khan Studs for 24,000euros in November; needs marked improvement on this yard/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Left J Murtagh for 24,000euros in November; needs marked improvement on handicap debut
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

races.race_name: Chester Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Galilean Quality
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three runs in 2025, signing off with a 1m2f Pontefract novice win (soft) in October; gelded before another improved effort to win handicap at Southwell (1m4f, AW) last month, in just a two-horse race but against in-form rival Magnetude (who reopposes); up 3lb but more to give.
runners.comment: Progressive 2yo; won two-runner handicap at Southwell (1m4f, AW) from Magnetude on return

runners.name: Magnetude
runners.spotlight: Improved on handicap debut when scoring at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) in March; appeared to run well when second to Galilean Quality in two-runner race at Southwell (1m4f, AW) last month, which puts him into the each-way picture assuming that first-time headgear proves suitable.
runners.comment: 2nd to Galilean Quality in two-runner race latest (1m4f); new headgear; in the e-w picture

runners.name: Parisian Scholar
runners.spotlight: Often front-runner; 3-4 in handicaps last term, with first win on good to soft going but also a flop on good; back with two creditable supporting roles behind Moment Of Light in five-runner handicaps at Kempton (1m3f, AW) and it looks like he will be suited by 1m4f+; this race is more competitive, though, and he needs to find a bit extra, with cheekpieces enlisted.
runners.comment: Needs to find a bit extra (cheekpieces on) after supporting roles behind Moment Of Light

runners.name: High Storm
runners.spotlight: Best efforts on first two starts this season, notably when winning from the front in a maiden at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) in March; fading into fifth of eight when 6-4 favourite for handicap debut at Newmarket (1m2f, good) three weeks ago was disappointing, but good chance he will stay 1m4f.
runners.comment: Won 1m2f maiden at Doncaster; faded when 6-4 favourite for 1m2f handicap at Newmarket

runners.name: Mythical Bay
runners.spotlight: Didn't live up to market expectations in first two starts but much better when runner-up next time and then won a five-runner novice at Wolverhampton (upped to 9.4f, AW) in January; that win came by just a neck at 2-5 but the second scored next time and this one rallied as if 1m4f would help; gelded soon afterwards; first turf run since debut (soft); one to consider.
runners.comment: Won by a neck at 2-5 in a novice (9.4f, AW) on latest outing; gelded; one to consider.

runners.name: Moment Of Light
runners.spotlight: Promise on turf (good to firm/good) as 2yo; gelded and tongue tied when justifying favouritism in five-runner handicaps over 1m3f on Kempton AW this February (fairly smoothly) and April (much harder work, by short head); up another 4lb and back to turf but 1m4f+ will help his hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: Dual handicap winner at Kempton (1m3f, AW; five ran); 1m4f+ will help his hat-trick bid

runners.name: Be The Standard
runners.spotlight: Couldn't justify favouritism on debut and bumped into the subsequent 2,000 Guineas winner next time, but made no mistake at Thirsk (1m, good) in August; flopped when favourite for five-runner nursery at Newcastle (1m, AW) in October but gelded since; sire gets plenty who stay; not dismissed.
runners.comment: 1m Thirsk novice winner who flopped when favourite for Newcastle nursery (1m, AW); gelded

runners.name: Oratorical
runners.spotlight: Substantial improvement to win novice at Nottingham in October, followed by creditable sixth of seven in French Listed race (both 1m on heavy); however, eased right down from 4f out on handicap/seasonal debut at Newmarket (1m2f, good; 22-1) three weeks ago, after which it's hard to approach him with confidence.
runners.comment: Eased right down from 4f out on handicap/seasonal debut at Newmarket (1m2f, good; 22-1)

runners.name: Carwyn
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in two of his three maiden races in Ireland but well beaten at 20-1 for handicap debut (1m, yielding to soft); has changed hands and left Ger Lyons; mixed pedigree for stamina (by Derby winner out of 5f winner); makes his seasonal debut for new connections.
runners.comment: Well beaten at 20-1 for handicap debut (1m); makes seasonal debut for new connections

races.race_name: Virgin Bet Daily Price Boosts "National Hunt" Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Loriko
runners.spotlight: Showed some fairly useful form last autumn but his handicap debut in December was disappointing and he was again below par when a short-odds second in Plumpton maiden (2m, good) last month; should be fine over this longer trip but has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Showed some fairly useful form in the autumn but comes here with a point to prove
runners.owner: Cherry Knoll Farm, M Ward & D Skelton

runners.name: Katarcice
runners.spotlight: Placed in two Irish points last spring and on both rules starts in March and April, latterly when second here (2m, good) on hurdle debut; ought to be suited by this longer trip and has less to prove than his rival.
runners.comment: Second over 2m here on last month's hurdling debut and this longer trip ought to suit

races.race_name: Virgin Bet Daily Extra Places Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Big Boy Barney
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over fences at second attempt, with very comfortable win in four-runner C&D (good) contest in March; again ran well when third at Southwell (2m4f, good; albeit finished tired) last month and is sure to be suited by the return to this track.
runners.comment: Won very comfortably over C&D in March and again ran well at Southwell last month

runners.name: Ice In The Veins
runners.spotlight: 2-7 over hurdles last season; raced too freely and disappointed when favourite for a valuable series final last month but makes chasing debut from a good mark; big player if he gets into a good jumping rhythm.
runners.comment: 2-7 over hurdles last season; makes chasing debut from a good mark; interesting

runners.name: Pep Talking
runners.spotlight: Took form to another new level when winning by 10l from the front at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) last month and is now 7-17 over fences (includes course win); today's trip-and-ground combination will also suit; respected.
runners.comment: Career-best form when winning by 10l at Chepstow last month; now 7-17 over fences

runners.name: William Cody
runners.spotlight: Gained three of four chase wins over C&D and returns here after going close on final two starts last season (2m4f/2m3f; wore cheekpieces last time); holds no secrets from the handicapper but will be fine on the forecast good ground and has obvious claims.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; returns here after going close on last two outings; obvious claims

runners.name: Koapey
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo; three non-completions have followed last October's promising chase/handicap debut but he was running fairly well before penultimate-fence fall in 2m Chepstow race won by Pep Talking last month; moves up in trip with Sean Bowen enlisted here; still has plenty of time to get career back on track.
runners.comment: Three non-completions have followed his promising chase debut; can still get back on track

runners.name: Hurricane Bay
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped if judged on his two good-ground wins in cheekpieces last spring (2m7f/2m5f) but has been well below best (in various headgear) in recent months; ran well for a long way before weakening quickly at Market Rasen (3m, good) last time and might benefit from this drop back in trip but others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way over 3m last time but has been well below his best for a while now

races.race_name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Mares' Handicap Chase (Queen Boudicca Mares' Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Kit's Coty
runners.spotlight: 1-10 over fences, the win gained on last season's reappearance at Ludlow (3m, good; Another Day Out second) in October; not in same form again since but now 2lb below that mark, and might be boosted by new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Rather disappointing since October's Ludlow win but might be boosted by cheepieces

runners.name: Another Day Out
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner (once over C&D); lost her way over fences during the winter but battled well for narrow hurdle win here (3m1f, good; front pair well clear) in March and reverts to this code on a good mark.
runners.comment: Battled well for course hurdle win in March and returns to chasing on a good mark

runners.name: Lady Jago
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner (2m4f-3m1f) on good ground for Dan Skelton in spring 2025; generally very disappointing for current stable since the autumn, including when back on a sound surface for last two starts, but has become too well handicapped to rule out.
runners.comment: Generally disappointing for current stable but now too well handicapped to rule out

runners.name: Catwalk Girl
runners.spotlight: Won small-field maiden point last spring and showed some promise in maiden/novice hurdles this year; fell mid-race on recent handicap hurdle debut but remains unexposed under rules and Max Comley is evidently keen to switch her to chasing.
runners.comment: Point winner; fell mid-race on recent handicap hurdle debut; still unexposed under rules
runners.owner: The Galloping Dentist At 45 The Green

races.race_name: Virgin Bet Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Notnowlinda
runners.spotlight: Yet to match peak form since returning from long absence in the autumn but her third in first-time cheekpieces at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good to soft) in late March was a pretty good effort, and she'll be fine on the forecast good ground here.
runners.comment: Ran well when third in first-time cheekpieces in March and good ground will suit

runners.name: Mermaids Cave
runners.spotlight: 7-27 over hurdles, including a good-ground C&D win; not firing on all cylinders since last autumn but wasn't beaten far when fifth of seven in refitted cheekpieces at Fakenham (2m4f, good) last month and continues to slip down the weights.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when fifth at Fakenham last month and continues to slip down the weights

runners.name: Mary
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with front-running win at Warwick (2m3f, good) in September and caught the eye at Stratford (2m, good) last month, after a break, making good headway from a long way off the pace before fading into fifth on the run-in; back up in trip here and it will be interesting to see whether she reverts to much more positive tactics.
runners.comment: Won from the front in September and returned from break with encouraging run last month

runners.name: No No Fizz
runners.spotlight: Looked back in good nick when scoring over C&D (good) in March but ran poorly and was pulled up at Uttoxeter last month (trainer blamed the good to soft ground); has become hard to predict.
runners.comment: Back in good nick when scoring over C&D in March but pulled up since; hard to predict

runners.name: Hope Rising
runners.spotlight: Progressive over hurdles in early part of last season, winning three times on good ground (2m-2m1f); absent since blotting copybook in the autumn (whipped round at start and refused to race) and not bred to benefit from this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Three wins in early part of last season; off since refusing to race in October; new trip

races.race_name: Virgin Bet Supports Safe Gambling Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Braganza Bay
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner; not at best since returning from one-year absence in November and was particularly disappointing when pulled up over fences last month; needs a revival but is back hurdling on a tempting mark.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; not at best since returning from one-year break in November; on good mark

runners.name: Raby Mere
runners.spotlight: Back on song in refitted cheekpieces at Southwell (3m, good) in March, his third start for Caroline Fryer, and the form of his respectable fourth at Fakenham (2m7f, good) last month was well advertised by the winner there on Monday; not yet fully exposed as a stayer; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Back on song in refitted cheekpieces towards end of last season; probably in the mix
runners.owner: C J Underwood And Caroline Fryer

runners.name: Greedy Smith
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles but responded well to first-time cheekpieces and belied 33-1 odds when second of 13 at Exeter (2m5f, good) last month; leading contender over this longer trip if the new headgear continues to help.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles but ran well in defeat when cheekpieces were added last month

runners.name: Indian Sunbird
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner last year, latterly over C&D (good) in October; absent since lesser effort here in November, though, and will need to resume in prime form to have a chance off his current mark.
runners.comment: Two wins in 2025, the latter over C&D; returns from break on fairly tough mark, though

runners.name: Moviddy
runners.spotlight: Placed in valuable series final over fences at Fakenham (2m5f, good to soft) in January but rest of last season's form was disappointing and it's hard to predict whether she'll be in the right mood for this return to hurdling; tried blinkers when pulled up last time; usual cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: Placed in valuable chase in January but otherwise disappointing last season; back hurdling
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate & Miss J Wignall

races.race_name: Virgin Bet Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Category 3 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Meli Melo d'Angos
runners.spotlight: First foal from a French 2m4f/2m5f chase winner; ran well to a point but dropped out rather tamely and was beaten about 24l when eighth of 11 at Hereford (good) last month; needs that run to have brought him on.
runners.comment: Dropped out rather tamely and was beaten about 24l when eighth at Hereford last month

runners.name: St Julie
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; half-sister to St Irene, who won a Listed mares' hurdle for connections last season, and a point winner; dam fair 2m1f/2m6f hurdle winner; one of two newcomers representing the stable.
runners.comment: Half-sister to St Irene, who won a Listed mares' hurdle for connections last season

runners.name: St Zoe
runners.spotlight: 34,000euros foal; third foal; half-sister to a point winner; dam unraced half-sister to useful jumpers Carrigmartin and City Slicker; riding arrangements suggest St Julie may be the stable's main hope.
runners.comment: Third foal from unraced half-sister to two useful jumpers; may be stable's second string

runners.name: As You Were
runners.spotlight: £22,000 3yo; second foal; dam runner-up in a point, half-sister to 2m4f hurdle winner Underdefloorboards; trainer 0-10 in bumpers during last five seasons (all unplaced).
runners.comment: £22,000 3yo; second foal; dam runner-up in a point; yard not known for winning bumpers

runners.name: Island Getaway
runners.spotlight: Out of a well-connected French hurdle winner; showed his inexperience and made only a mildly encouraging debut when well-held fifth of eight over C&D (good) last month but improvement is possible.
runners.comment: Made only a mildly encouraging debut when well-held fifth of eight over C&D last month

races.race_name: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Cameley Days
runners.spotlight: Won twice on slower than good last October; dropped right away on her return to action at Leicester 12 days ago (6f, good to firm; Dragon God third); well drawn to attack back at 5f but slower ground would be preferable.
runners.comment: Two wins last autumn on slower than good; well beaten on her return; ground a slight query

runners.name: Hello Luna
runners.spotlight: C&D win last June off a 2lb higher mark; has run some good races on AW this year, most recently finishing a close third of 11 at Kempton four weeks ago (6f); solid contender.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 2lb higher last June; in good order on AW this year; solid claims

runners.name: Manhattan Chute
runners.spotlight: Sprung a 50-1 surprise at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January but he has finished last in four starts since; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Finished last in four runs following his shock Lingfield win in January; hard to recommend
runners.owner: J May, R L Field, W Trenchard, J Pook

runners.name: Barnsnape Boy
runners.spotlight: Two wins last August, over 5f at Lingfield (good ground; cheekpieces first time) and 5.3f at Brighton (good); ended that campaign quietly and he returns from 203 days off without the headgear.
runners.comment: Last summer's two wins came in cheekpieces which are absent today; others appeal more

runners.name: She Went Whoosh
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 31 starts; recent efforts don't suggest she is primed to double her tally; now tongue tied.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate and not sure she's in the form required to improve it

runners.name: Cabeza De Llave
runners.spotlight: Two of his three turf wins have come over C&D; solid return to action at Chelmsford in February but he took a backward step at Southwell 12 days ago; William Carson replaces a 7lb claimer; not discounted.
runners.comment: Two-time C&D winner; on a feasible mark and he should have a big run in him

runners.name: Tilsworth Ony Ta
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner but has been short of his best on AW this year and the return to turf made no difference at Bath 32 days ago; withdrawn after bolting to post at Doncaster a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Not at best so far this year; too much to prove for comfort

runners.name: Dragon God
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been with Tony Carroll for long and there has been clear promise in two runs since returning to turf, latterly when a close third at Leicester (6f, good to firm) 12 days ago; runs off the same mark and has the assistance of Rossa Ryan for the first time; high on the list.
runners.comment: 0-8 but clear promise in two turf runs for Tony Carroll; one to take seriously

runners.name: Under Curfew
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is on a losing run of 22; knocking on the door on AW when last seen and he won't mind getting back on the grass; has made the running but it is not a prerequisite; could go well.
runners.comment: On a losing run but won't mind conditions and he could go well back on turf

runners.name: Secret Handsheikh
runners.spotlight: Slipping down the weights and his latest Lingfield third (5f AW) wasn't a bad effort; today's return to the turf will help and he is the type to come good soon.
runners.comment: Slipping down the weights and his latest 3rd was encouraging; type to come good soon

runners.name: Honour Your Dreams
runners.spotlight: C&D win last May and followed up at Lingfield (6f; both good to firm) one week later; now 14lb lower so he has handicapping claims, for all his recent efforts need bettering; stable also runs Cabeza De Llave.
runners.comment: C&D winner who has slipped down the weights; one of two runners for Michael Attwater

races.race_name: Judge Law Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Starryfield
runners.spotlight: Fair form when twice placed in 5f/6f novices at Newcastle in the winter; seemed to find the step up to 7f stretching his stamina when seventh there on his handicap debut last time so he's a player back in trip .
runners.comment: Twice placed at Newcastle before stamina stretched over 7f there; a player back in trip

runners.name: Jazl
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when a debut second in 6f Thirsk maiden in August, but failed to build on it when third of four in 6f Brighton maiden the following month; since gelded and this son of No Nay Never is worth another chance on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Promising Thirsk second but failed to build on it at Brighton after; worth another chance

runners.name: Miss Nightcap
runners.spotlight: £40,000 yearling, 1,000gns 2yo; sister to 6f/7f winner Under The Twilight (RPR 95); dam, 6f winner (RPR 86), is a half-sister to 1m winner Border Owl, out of 6f 2yo Listed winning half-sister to 1m Listed winner Edinburgh Knight; Twilight son filly who has already had a wind op ahead of her debut; the market should prove an accurate guide.
runners.comment: Twilight son filly who has already had a wind op ahead of debut; market can guide
runners.owner: E S-O Racing Club And Elaine Chivers

runners.name: Stolen
runners.spotlight: Still looked in need of the experience but she stepped up on her debut fifth when fourth of five in 6f AW Lingfield maiden 23 days ago; this daughter of Rumble Inthe Jungle may do better still.
runners.comment: Still looked in need of experience when fourth at Lingfield latest; may do better still

runners.name: Swiped
runners.spotlight: Hinted at promise when fifth over C&D and in 7f Leicester novice last summer; since gelded and this son of Too Darn Hot could take a step forward on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise at 2yrs; gelded and could take a step forward on his seasonal return

races.race_name: Padel Maidenhead Anniversary Cup Handicap

runners.name: The Hare Rail
runners.spotlight: Yet to register a victory but he arrives in decent nick, fading late when seventh in 1m2f Lingfield handicap 17 days ago; not out of things back in trip in a change of headgear.
runners.comment: Yet to register a victory but he arrives in decent nick; not out of things back in trip
runners.owner: Mrs Muriel Forward & Dr G C Forward

runners.name: Narmar
runners.spotlight: In fine form last summer, scoring at Ffos Las (1m) and signing off with an excellent second over 1m2f here; set a lot to do on his return from eight months off when tenth in 7f Thirsk handicap 19 days ago; should strip sharper now and he's very much one to consider back up in trip.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in fine form; off eight months before Thirsk tenth; very much one to consider

runners.name: Risen Again
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m2f winner at his best for Alice Haynes, but he's failed to beat a rival in handicaps at Lingfield (1m5f) and Bath (1m) in 2026; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in handicaps at Lingfield and Bath in 2026; others appeal more

runners.name: Under The Sun
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing run at Lingfield (1m2f) in February and backed it up with a solid fourth of 12 in 1m2f handicap there the following month; considered.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield and backed it up with a solid fourth of 12 there; considered

runners.name: Norflondonforever
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who took a big step forward in first-time tongue-tie when landing 1m Kempton handicap last April; absent since, though, and headgear is left off for his comeback.
runners.comment: Tongue tied when winning at Kempton last April; absent since and headgear is left off here

runners.name: Great Blasket
runners.spotlight: Bagged a pair of 1m handicaps at Chepstow and Sandown last summer; failed to fire on the AW over the winter but yard is going well so he could bounce back returned to turf after a break.
runners.comment: Failed to fire on AW over the winter but yard is going well so he could bounce back

runners.name: Crimson Road
runners.spotlight: A dual 1m2f scorer in 2024 for Simon and Ed Crisford; failed to fire for Susan Corbett last year, though, and he came in a below-par fifth for his new yard in 1m Bath handicap last month; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Failed to fire for Susan Corbett last year; below-par fifth for new yard at Bath on return

runners.name: Zarathos
runners.spotlight: 0-17 but he's a consistent sort and posted a solid fourth of 12 in 1m Wetherby handicap 11 days ago; ought to be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: 0-17 but a solid fourth in 1m Wetherby handicap 11 days ago; ought to be thereabouts
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Kodi Fire
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick over C&D in August but he ended the season with a couple of below-par efforts at Wolverhampton and Newbury; needs to hit the ground running on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: C&D winner but ended the season below par; needs to hit the ground running on return

runners.name: Canopus
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m maiden for Ed Walker; last of 13 for his new yard on his handicap debut at Lingfield (1m2f) in February, though, and well held over hurdles at Kempton since.
runners.comment: Last for new yard on h'cap debut at Lingfield in February; well held over hurdles since

runners.name: Dutch Kingdom
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 6f-1m AW winner at his best for Darryll Holland, but he failed to beat a rival on his yard debut in 7f Kempton handicap in November; needs to take a big step forward after a break.
runners.comment: Failed to beat rival on yard debut at Kempton in November; needs to take big step forward

runners.name: Symphony Of Joy
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in a trio of runs in 2025, keeping on into fourth in 7f Leicester novice in October; since gelded and this son of Ulysses could have more to offer now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Promise shown in three runs in 2025; since gelded; could have more to offer on h'cap debut

races.race_name: Best Odds Guaranteed At greatbet.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Stintino Sunset
runners.spotlight: Consistent mare who posted a good third of eight to Belle Amie in 1m4f classified event at Wolverhampton in February; this C&D winner needs considering.
runners.comment: Reliable mare; good third at Wolverhampton in February; this C&D winner needs considering

runners.name: Fullforward
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who resumed from five months off with a solid fourth of eight in 1m4f Lingfield handicap a week ago; not out of things off the same mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; solid fourth at Lingfield a week ago; not out of things off the same mark

runners.name: Moon Over The Sea
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW over the winter before resuming winning ways in 11.5f apprentice handicap at Bath ten days ago; escapes a penalty, so holds leading claims.
runners.comment: Won apprentice handicap at Bath latest; escapes a penalty, so holds leading claims

runners.name: Gearing's Point
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2023 but she comes here in decent nick, fifth of 12 in 1m4f classified event at Lingfield in March; possibilities.
runners.comment: Winless since 2023 but she comes here in decent nick; possibilities

runners.name: Belle Amie
runners.spotlight: Reliable maiden for David Menuisier in 2025; made a winning start for David Simcock in 1m4f classified event at Wolverhampton in February and she must enter calculations after a break back in handicap company.
runners.comment: Winning start for David Simcock at Wolverhampton in February; must enter calculations

runners.name: Douglas Dc
runners.spotlight: Still without a win on the Flat and he's been off since a below-par 11th over hurdles at Newbury in November; needs to take a big step forward after a break.
runners.comment: Still without a win on the Flat and off since below-par effort over hurdles in November

runners.name: Cribbins
runners.spotlight: Little show in a pair of bumpers and it's been a similar story in a trio of Flat runs, last of 13 in 1m Kempton novice last month; blinkered/tongue tied for his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Little show in a trio of Flat runs; blinkered/tongue tied for his handicap debut

runners.name: Majestic Leo
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m2f maiden at his best for Ralph Beckett but he's yet to offer much for his current yard, beating just two in 1m handicap at Bath last month; not easy to warm to.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much for his current yard, beating just two in 1m handicap at Bath last month

runners.name: Almavillalobas
runners.spotlight: Yet to make the frame in her eight runs to date at up to 7f, retried in a visor when tenth in 7f Lingfield classified event in February; takes a big step up in trip now.
runners.comment: Yet to make the frame in her eight runs to date; takes a big step up in trip now
runners.owner: C Seeney, S Holden, Mildean , M Robinson

runners.name: Koko Blue
runners.spotlight: Yet to win and she ended 2025 below form on the AW for William Muir and Chris Grassick, only tenth in 2m Wolverhampton handicap in December; needs this yard switch to spark a resurgence.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 below form on the AW; needs this yard switch to spark a resurgence

runners.name: Hello Sweety
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced on the Flat in France, but she enjoyed a very good winter for her current yard over hurdles, bagging a pair of 2m handicaps at Fakenham; since undergone wind surgery and can make her presence felt back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner over the winter; lightly raced and can make her presence felt

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Happy Chandler
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 17th attempt when making all at Leicester 12 days ago (1m2f, good to firm); up 5lb but the latest run was his first at the trip and he may be able to build on it.
runners.comment: Off the mark upped to 1m2f at Leicester last month; 5lb higher but may do better still

runners.name: Bizou
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent nine-race maiden; ran well on her seasonal reappearance (9.4f, AW) and a reproduction would see her on the premises.
runners.comment: Close second on her seasonal return in March (9.4f, AW); in the mix with a repeat

runners.name: Haveagobeau
runners.spotlight: Took an age to get off the mark, finally opening his account at the 31st attempt in a 1m2f handicap at Lingfield (AW) in January; four runs since, twice finishing second before recording another Lingfield success six weeks ago; still unexposed at this trip and he is effective on turf.
runners.comment: Two wins and two seconds from his last five starts; not fully exposed over 1m2f

runners.name: Read All About It
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; returned to form when third of eight in a Wolverhampton handicap in February (9.4f; cheekpieces first time); still has time to deliver on her early promise and neither track nor ground should be an issue; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Back to form in first-time cheekpieces in February (9.4f); good chance if building on it

runners.name: Celebrating Ethel
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to win a 9.4f 0-50 classified race at Wolverhampton in March (for Rossa Ryan); not so good when seventh behind Haveagobeau at Lingfield (1m2f) six weeks ago; others are stronger for win purposes.
runners.comment: AW win in March (9.4f); not so good behind Haveagobeau one week later; others appeal more

runners.name: Hawaiian King
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on his turf debut at Bath last month; his best AW form brings him into the reckoning but there are risks attached; new blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Bits of form on AW but tailed off on last month's turf debut; new headgear tried today
runners.owner: Aura Racing, Dave Brown, Joe Lane

runners.name: Beta Reader
runners.spotlight: Wouldn't judge him too harshly on his latest effort (1m; Class 5) and his penultimate run didn't see him to best effect either; has shown promise on turf; not discounted.
runners.comment: Has had excuses on his last two starts; promise on turf for former yard; not discounted

runners.name: Fighting Poet
runners.spotlight: All three of his wins have come at Brighton; well treated on last summer's ready 1m2f win but returning from an absence perhaps not the time to catch him.
runners.comment: 1m2f Brighton win last summer brings him into it; may come on for this seasonal return

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Dark Sun
runners.spotlight: 0-16 but she has had some near misses along the way, notably at Wolverhampton in February (6f) when beaten a nose by a thriving rival; not so good back on turf at Leicester 12 days ago; may have to wait longer to shed her maiden tag.
runners.comment: Exposed 16-race maiden but she looks capable of winning a small race; perhaps not this one

runners.name: My Mate Kev
runners.spotlight: Now 9lb lower than for his Newmarket win last May (6f, good); little to shout about since and unlikely to get an easy time of things up front.
runners.comment: 9lb lower than for Newmarket win last May but there has been little to shout about since

runners.name: Phoenix Moon
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins to her name but she is yet to show comparable form on turf, including when a beaten favourite at Nottingham 11 days ago; may come good on the grass at some point.
runners.comment: 4 AW wins but not shown comparable form on turf; still has time to get it right on grass

runners.name: Just Jump
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when third at Lingfield (6f, AW) on his stable debut in February; hasn't fared so well since, with slow starts a recurring theme, and he will need luck in running if he blows the start from stall 1; cheekpieces the headgear of choice today.
runners.comment: Has the ability to defy this mark but he's an habitual slow starter and he'll need luck

runners.name: Twirler
runners.spotlight: Two turf wins last summer, including C&D, and she added a 7f handicap at Kempton to her tally in February; back to a winning mark and can't be discounted, but fast ground is a slight concern.
runners.comment: C&D winner; on a good mark and capable of going well if the ground is no issue

runners.name: Midnight Call
runners.spotlight: Promise on turf last summer and she has made good progress on AW since November, winning five times at Wolverhampton; came up short off this mark 16 days ago and the handicapper may now have her measure.
runners.comment: Five Wolverhampton wins since November; handicapper may now have caught up

runners.name: Dreambird Dolly
runners.spotlight: Exposed 16-race maiden; returns from six months off and others have much more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Exposed 16-race maiden; absent for six months and easy enough to look elsewhere
runners.owner: T Beeches, C Stone, J Gallagher

runners.name: Margorie
runners.spotlight: Sprung a surprise in a soft-ground C&D maiden last September; no impact in handicaps and although last time at Haydock (cheekpieces first time) was a bit better, others are more appealing.
runners.comment: Shock C&D win last September came on soft ground; little impact in handicaps since

runners.name: Candy Warhol
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; arrives on the back of two second-place finishes over 5f on the AW; 0-8 on turf but is effective on good to firm; not sure to benefit from the return to 6f.
runners.comment: Front-runner who arrives in form; 0-8 on turf and not sure to benefit from return to 6f
runners.owner: Homecroft Wealth, Paul J Dixon & Partners

runners.name: May Encounter
runners.spotlight: Yet to build on the promise of her 2yo debut, but she now tackles her first Class 6 handicap on her return from a seven-month break; still has time to do better.
runners.comment: Not built on her 2yo debut effort, but drops to Class 6 company on her seasonal return

runners.name: Capote's Dream
runners.spotlight: Three of his wins have come over C&D but the last victory was back in August 2023; out of sorts on AW over the winter but he drops into a Class 6 and if he is to stage a revival, it is likely to be here.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; out of sorts on AW when last seen; now drops to Class 6

races.race_name: Wexfordraces.ie Beginners Chase

runners.name: Farfromnowhere
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler whose two wins were gained over longer trips than this; ran only twice over fences last season; promising effort behind Kala Conti over 2m5f at Cork, well beaten in fourth in a Limerick Grade 3 contest over 2m6f; in rear in the Martin Pipe at Cheltenham; 2m looks a bit short but could prevail.
runners.comment: Decent hurdler over longer trips, solid chase form, 2m looks a bit short but could prevail

runners.name: Karoline Banbou
runners.spotlight: 130-rated hurdler; fair efforts on first two chase runs, though was beaten by longer-priced stablemate Port Joulain at Fairyhouse in January; beaten falling at the last in a Leopardstown event that has been well advertised by the 8l runner-up Come Walk With Me in landing handicaps at Fairyhouse and Punchestown.
runners.comment: Second on both completed starts over fences, bids to make amends now for Leopardstown fall

runners.name: Kilgame
runners.spotlight: Second over hurdles at Auteuil in March 2024; ran only twice last season; shaped well at Leopardstown last December on reappearance; appeared not to stay the near-2m3f trip at Naas in January; got his career back on track when winning a 23-runner maiden hurdle at Navan in March.
runners.comment: Got his career back on track when winning a 23-runner maiden hurdle at Navan in March

runners.name: Did I Ask You That
runners.spotlight: Second in a bumper in 2022; third to The Big Westerner in a 2m6f Punchestown maiden hurdle in November 2024; off for a year; slipped up before three out when travelling well in the same maiden last season; reported blowing hard after a tame run at Tramore; same excuse offered at Naas; seems very risky.
runners.comment: Reported blowing hard after his two most recent outings, seems very risky

runners.name: Glenashling
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner early last year; fair form over fences last season, best effort a second placing at Punchestown in November when in front of the useful Coral River; runner-up in a six-runner contest at Cork on handicap debut; officially rated 11lb inferior to Karoline Banbou.
runners.comment: Second in a six-runner handicap at Cork, officially rated 11lb inferior to Karoline Banbou

runners.name: Mister Pink
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper and a 2m4f maiden hurdle for Willie Mullins; after a long absence, beaten from three out on stable/chasing debut in a Cork race won by Mullins with subsequent Punchestown handicap winner Funiculi Funicula.
runners.comment: Won a bumper and a 2m4f maiden hurdle for Willie Mullins, well beaten on stable debut

runners.name: Mercury Mission
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner (rated 113); unseated early on stable and chase debut at Downpatrick; ran a fine race from the front  at 80-1 when second to the useful Royal Hollow at Clonmel last month; should be able to pick up a race over fences on that evidence but this looks like a more competitive race.
runners.comment: Fine effort when second at 80-1 at Clonmel, this looks like a more competitive race

runners.name: Mick Collins
runners.spotlight: Won 4yo maiden hurdle at Punchestown last June on good ground; placed in novice events at Bellewstown and Galway; failed to sustain that form in two outings before being left off in the autumn; Flat win over 1m4f came after a lengthy break, so could go well here.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler, Flat win over 1m4f came after a lengthy break so could go well here

runners.name: Faulty
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Down Royal maiden hurdle in March 2024; reported coughing after a poor run the following month; off for almost two years before chasing debut at Naas two months ago, jumped untidily and never in contention.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles in 2024, jumped untidily and never in contention at Naas on chasing debut
runners.owner: Albert Dravins & Exors of the Late Eamonn Scanlon

runners.name: Fenian Maxios
runners.spotlight: Has run five times over hurdles; his much-improved second at Thurles in March seems to have been a flash in the pan, since he has struggled in two subsequent outings.
runners.comment: His much-improved second at Thurles in March seems to have been a flash in the pan

runners.name: Jerrari
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back winner of handicap hurdle at Roscommon and Galway last summer, both at around 2m on good ground; testing conditions were against him at Navan in February; harder to excuse a poor Downpatrick run; unplaced at Kilbeggan last time; could make a summer chaser but will probably need to gain experience.
runners.comment: Won twice over hurdles, could make a summer chaser, will probably need to gain experience

runners.name: Wild Bill Hickok
runners.spotlight: Won a Listed Hurdle for 3yos at Auteuil in April last year; badly out of sorts in France subsequently; a bit better on debut for Ciaran Murphy in a handicap hurdle at Aintree; best watched on first attempt over fences.
runners.comment: Lost form over hurdles in France, best watched on his first attempt over fences

races.race_name: Visitwexford.ie Handicap Chase

runners.name: In The Trenches
runners.spotlight: Long-standing maiden; has run well in defeat over fences and hurdles, including runner-up in 2m2f Cork handicap chase, most recently beaten under 2l when fifth of 8 in steadily-run 2m Tramore novice handicap chase last month;  ground to suit and definite contender.
runners.comment: Not beaten far at Tramore recently off this mark; contender on favoured ground

runners.name: Courtbrack Boy
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt over hurdles at Clonmel (2m, heavy) in February; beaten off 7lb higher mark subsequently but good run on chase debut last month in 2m Tramore handicap, making most until outpointed from last by strong favourite (yielding); 2lb rise okay, likely major player.
runners.comment: Chase debut second at Tramore very encouraging; 2lb rise okay, likely major player
runners.owner: Michael P Murray & Timothy R Murray

runners.name: Midnight Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner last year is 0-9 chasing but some good placed efforts at around 3m, early last season; halfway faller on return to track in March and now takes a marked drop in trip.
runners.comment: Remains on a competitive mark but best form is over much further

runners.name: Butter Fingers
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle wins came in 2024; 0-4 chasing this past season (blood in nostrils fourth occasion) and back hurdling the last twice, notably when placed in 2m Naas handicap off 110; scope for improvement off this 7lb lower chase mark.
runners.comment: Scope for improvement off this 7lb lower chase mark

runners.name: Pahlavi
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough success over hurdles came at Downpatrick last summer (2m2f, good), absent since midfield in 2m Limerick handicap in October, having had an easy lead; only second chase start (well held in Roscommon handicap last summer).
runners.comment: Absent since October and opposable on second chase start

runners.name: L'Amiral Fromentin
runners.spotlight: First real effort of note on latest when decent third (3l off runner-up Courtbrack Boy) in 2m Tramore novice handicap chase last month (yielding); 5yo seems to be on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Recent Tramore third encouraging but bit to find with runner-up Courtbrack Boy

runners.name: Half A Chance
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in 2m Limerick maiden hurdle two years ago; consistent placed form in handicap hurdles last year, well below best last month at Plumpton; straight into handicap company for chase debut and inexperience a concern.
runners.comment: Straight into handicap company for chase debut and inexperience a concern

runners.name: Let Him B
runners.spotlight: Progressive handicap hurdler this past season, off the mark over 2m5f at Down Royal in March (yielding) and better again following month off 11lb higher when clear second to a ready winner over 2m4f at Limerick (soft-to-heavy); improving 6yo could make an immediate impact chasing here although drop in trip may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Improving 6yo could make an immediate impact chasing although drop in trip not be ideal

runners.name: Soldante
runners.spotlight: Tramore maiden hurdle winner in late 2023 and best of two recent runs over fences came at same venue last month when rallying third (In The Trenches fifth) in 2m novice handicap chase; could be capable of better over fences so one to consider.
runners.comment: Recent Tramore third was encouraging on only second chase start; one to consider
runners.owner: P McCormack & David P McCormack & Sarah Glynn

runners.name: Presenting Lad
runners.spotlight: Chase wins came at Roscommon and Tramore in 2024; well held in handicaps last year and returns from 7 months' absence off much-reduced mark.
runners.comment: Veteran returns from 7 months' absence off much-reduced mark

runners.name: Tequila Talkin'
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle win came here (2m4f, good) last summer; softer ground in recent outings wouldn't have been ideal so potential improver now back on better ground, having not quite got home when fourth in 2m4f novice handicap on chase debut here last month (well-backed); drops in trip.
runners.comment: Well-backed on last month's chase debut here when fourth over 2m4f; drops in trip

runners.name: Ernie From Nurney
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back handicap hurdle winner in April 2024 on soft-to-heavy; only glimpses of his best form since, placed in Thurles handicap chase early last year best run; pulled up over hurdles at Naas in February on latest.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner but just sporadic glimpses of form nowadays

runners.name: Shannon Bank
runners.spotlight: Maiden took her mark to a career-high when coming from well back to chase home a clear winner in 2m1f Killarney  handicap hurdle in October (yielding); never recovered from bad blunder at Sligo later same month in first-time cheekpieces; has since left Gavin Cromwell and debuts for new yard on chase debut.
runners.comment: Ex Gavin Cromwell; absent since October and debuts over fences for new yard

races.race_name: Wexford Town Handicap Chase

runners.name: War Correspondent
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort showed decent form over fences last autumn, including third in a Punchestown Grade 3 novice; not an easy horse to win with and has to rebound now after a failure over hurdles at Tramore.
runners.comment: Not an easy horse to win with and has to rebound now after a failure at Tramore

runners.name: Dutch Schultz
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose type; most recent of two chase wins came in a 2m1f Killarney handicap chase in August 2024; best effort last year when fourth in a 24-runner handicap chase at the Punchestown festival; not a bad run at Fairyhouse after a break; however, Come On The Lads seems to be the yard's number one.
runners.comment: Not a bad run at Fairyhouse after a break, Come On The Lads seems to be yard's number one

runners.name: Kilbrin Rocco
runners.spotlight: Possibly value for more than the neck margin when winning at Tramore last month; only 4lb higher and is at the right end of the handicap here; every chance of getting in the mix.
runners.comment: Possibly value for more than the neck margin when winning at Tramore, only 4lb higher

runners.name: Come On The Lads
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler won a valuable prize at Galway last year; ran well at Fairyhouse on the first of two chase starts last autumn; returned from a break to win in good style at Limerick; leading chance despite 8lb rise.
runners.comment: Returned from a break to win in good style at Limerick, leading chance despite 8lb rise

runners.name: Crowsatedappletart
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner over hurdles/fences; won twice at this venue last July; likely to show the benefit of a recent hurdles run at Limerick; noteworthy on account of his ability to handle the track; good chance with a top claimer aboard.
runners.comment: Won twice at this venue last July, likely to show the benefit of a recent hurdles run

runners.name: An Peann Dearg
runners.spotlight: Both chase wins have been in 2m1f handicaps at Leopardstown; sights are lowered here after running in stronger company at that venue during the winter.
runners.comment: Sights are lowered here after running in stronger company at Leopardstown in the winter

runners.name: Trasna Na Pairce
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back hurdling wins at Clonmel last summer; also front-running second at same venue in a 2m3f novice handicap chase; wins were over further; on the positive side, the ground should be ideal for him.
runners.comment: Hurdle wins at Clonmel last season were over further, the ground should be ideal for him

runners.name: Cappucino
runners.spotlight: Won Listowel bumper and Punchestown maiden hurdle the season before last; best chase run yet when second at Tramore on New Year's Day; had an excuse when pulled up at Thurles last time; definite chance if restored to his best.
runners.comment: Had an excuse when pulled up at Thurles last time, definite chance if restored to his best

runners.name: Baltic Bird
runners.spotlight: Ground should be coming right for him now; probably needs further than this, and his lack of a recent run is a possible drawback.
runners.comment: Ground should be coming right for him now, probably needs further than this, no recent run

runners.name: Captain Conby
runners.spotlight: Should be sharper now following a comeback on the Flat; had very useful form in 2023; might just need another race or two to become competitive again.
runners.comment: Should be sharper now following a comeback on the Flat, had very useful form in 2023

races.race_name: Purple And Gold Beginners Chase

runners.name: Hiddenvalley Lake
runners.spotlight: Classy hurdler rated (154) some way superior to these, career highlight when winning 3m Grade 1 Liverpool Hurdle at Aintree just over a year ago; only fifth when attempting repeat win there last month but looks a class apart from these rivals if anyway fluent in his jumping (wasn't that on sole previous chase start back in December 2023); interesting jockey booking.
runners.comment: Let down by jumping on chase debut back in late 2023 but Grade 1-winning hurdler since

runners.name: Eclipse Chaser
runners.spotlight: 121-rated hurdler who won 2m5f Navan maiden early last season (yielding) but struggled in handicaps, too keen when pulled up at Fairyhouse last time; half-brother to Grade 2-winning chaser Westerner Lady so fair chance he'll take to chasing.
runners.comment: Disappointed in handicap hurdles last term; could make a better chaser

runners.name: Long Branch
runners.spotlight: 118-rated hurdler won 2m7f Thurles novice last season (yielding) and chased home a clear winner in 3m novice here last month on similar ground; left with plenty to do in first-time cheekpieces last week when staying on into a remote fourth over inadequate 2m4f at the Punchestown festival; needs to improve for switch to fences to feature in a major way.
runners.comment: 118-rated hurdler needs to improve for switch to fences to feature in a major way

runners.name: American Jukebox
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 145-rated chaser American Mike; placed in 2m4f bumper and off the mark hurdling when making virtually all for runaway win in ordinary 2m6f Tramore maiden last season (soft); safely held twice afterwards in handicaps and returns from a break for chase debut.
runners.comment: American Mike's half-brother rated 117 over hurdles; more likely needed on chase debut

runners.name: One Big Boum
runners.spotlight: Progressive handicap hurdler last season took his mark to 125 after wins over 2m4f at Navan and Fairyhouse (heavy); 2m trip inadequate here on final start in March; upped in trip for chase debut and could go well if taking to fences.
runners.comment: Progressive hurdler upped in trip for chase debut and could go well if taking to fences

runners.name: D Art D Art
runners.spotlight: 125-rated hurdler was in good form 2024/25 season, winning twice and running well in 3m Pertemps Final at Cheltenham; well below that level in light campaign last season however and now tries fences.
runners.comment: Well below best hurdling last season and now debuts over fences

races.race_name: Buy Tickets Online Novice Hunters Chase

runners.name: Tiffiny's Girl
runners.spotlight: Placed in a maiden hurdle last season; made the frame in all four outings in points; looked progressive when winning a four-runner hunter chase at Cork by 12l, though her task was eased by sloppy jumping on the part of favourite Lucy Wang; seemed to enjoy better ground and may continue to improve
runners.comment: Won a four-runner contest at Cork by 12l, seemed to enjoy better ground, looks progressive

runners.name: Begger
