runners.spotlight: Point-to-point form is modest; however, he was in the process of running an excellent race when unseating at the last on his hunter debut at Cork; failed to sustain that improvement when fifth behind today's rival Kiwi De Cotte at Ballysteen 12 days ago.
runners.comment: In touch when exiting at the last in a hunter chase, held by Kiwi De Cotte on a recent run

runners.name: Black Dakota
runners.spotlight: Useful form in points since getting his jumping act together (fell twice last year); twice runner-up since Cragmore win in January; cheekpieces on now; fair chance if avoiding error.
runners.comment: Useful form in points, twice runner-up since Cragmore win in January, cheekpieces on now

runners.name: Kiwi de Cotte
runners.spotlight: Has won both completed starts in points, pulled up on the other two occasions; had Begger back in fifth when scoring at Ballysteen 12 days ago; stable in flying form in the last fortnight; Jack Hendrick claims a valuable 7lb.
runners.comment: Has won both completed starts in points, stable in flying form, valuable 7lb claimed

runners.name: Muskerry Rock
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on track for Mouse Morris and has a fair record in points, two wins and three places from six attempts; however, failed to make an impact at Limerick in December on his only hunter chase debut, down the field again at Tramore recently.
runners.comment: Two wins in points last autumn, more recent form has been less encouraging

runners.name: Occupational Force
runners.spotlight: One win and nine places in points; needs to find extra after Cork fifth;  top point-to-point rider Rob James is enjoying a great run of form and is now the best 5lb claimer in the business, having recently lost the right to take off 7lb.
runners.comment: One win and nine places in points, needs to find extra after Cork fifth, rider in top form

runners.name: Thehandsomecharmer
runners.spotlight: Beat the only other finisher at Oldcastle in March; 2 1/2l behind Black Dakota when fourth at Rathcannon; others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Beat the only other finisher at Oldcastle, 2 1/2l behind Black Dakota at Rathcannon

runners.name: Rilo Tang
runners.spotlight: Beat the useful Rongowright (beat Black Dakota recently) at Turtulla last autumn; needs to bounce back after a poor run in February.
runners.comment: Beat a useful sort at Turtulla last autumn, needs to bounce back after poor run on latest

races.race_name: Slaney Mares Point-To-Point INH Flat Race

runners.name: Wilton Mills Trier
runners.spotlight: Won on debut in a mares' maiden at Dromahane in December; runner-up finished second to good yardstick Dora D'Or (four points wins/hunter chase winner) on her next start; favourite in a similar race to this at Cork a month ago, finished a close third after running close to the pace; may put that experience to good use now.
runners.comment: Debut winner at Dromahane, may show the benefit of her run in a similar race at Cork

runners.name: Wait Awhile
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to Lucinda Russell's four-time winner Whistle Stop Tour; failed to complete twice before winning at Castlelands in May; well held by Dedudefromhouston on running at Ballynoe.
runners.comment: Won at Castlelands in May, well held by Dedudefromhouston on running at Ballynoe

runners.name: One Day In May
runners.spotlight: 12th foal; half-sister by Idaho to three winners; runner-up on her only two starts in points last season; again second at Ballynoe on return, went one better when favourite at Dromahane, beating a well-exposed rival; represents a strong trainer/rider combination; wears a hood.
runners.comment: Second on her first three starts, favourite when beating a well-exposed sort at Dromahane

runners.name: Five Shiros
runners.spotlight: £45,000 3yo; second foal; dam 1m2f Flat and 2m hurdle winner; beaten favourite on both starts in points; beaten when falling two out on debut; 10l second to Elderflower Tonic at Lingstown, winner was then beaten 10l in a bumper at Doncaster on British debut; represents a top producer.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in both outings in points, represents a top producer, not ruled out here

runners.name: Princess Aveen
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to two point winners; runner-up on her first two starts in points; seemed to have plenty in hand when winning a 15-runner contest at Fairyhouse 11 days ago; stable in form.
runners.comment: Seemed to have plenty in hand when winning a 15-runner race at Fairyhouse, stable in form

runners.name: Silent Temptation
runners.spotlight: Order Of St George mare from the stable of the champion-elect point-to-point trainer; has a claim as the form horse based on her defeat of Tiffiny's Girl at Lisronagh; 6l defeat at Ballyknock raises a doubt, though the winner was also an easy winner last May on her only previous start.
runners.comment: Beat subsequent hunter chase winner Tiffiny's Girl at Lisronagh, soundly beaten on latest

runners.name: Dedudefromhouston
runners.spotlight: First foal; by Idaho out of a hurdle winner by Sixties Icon; winner of two of her four starts in points, scoring readily on both occasions; a shade disappointing when second in the Gain Mares Final at Ballynoe, however, the winner is an experienced mare, and there was a 9l gap to the third.
runners.comment: Two emphatic wins in points before a Ballynoe defeat, one of the leading contenders

runners.name: Morning Express
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to Noel Meade's useful hurdle chase winner Idas Boy; second to the useful Lucy Wang (beat Deduefromhouston at Ballynoe) first time at Turtulla last November; beat a subsequent winner easily at Ballygogue in February; of some interest on belated racecourse debut
runners.comment: A latecomer to pointing this season, built on a promising debut when winning in February

runners.name: Generous Valentine
runners.spotlight: Decent pedigree but was well beaten in two bumpers in Britain in 2023 and has shown little in points.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two bumpers in Britain in 2023 and has shown little in points

runners.name: Miss Appleberry
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam placed in points, half-sister to six-time jumps winner Soll; ran twice in bumpers in Britain in 2024; won at Dungarvan in January on the second of two starts in points for Terence O'Brien; runner-up Tiffiny's Girl won a maiden hunter chase at Cork and bids to follow up in the 7.08 race.
runners.comment: Won at Dungarvan in January beating subsequent hunter chase winner Tiffiny's Girl

races.race_name: Racing Again 16th May (C & G) Point-To-Point INH Flat Race

runners.name: Fortysixnchange
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to a Swedish 6f dirt winner; dam Flat/hurdle winner; cost GBP26,000 in February after finishing third of six in a heavy-ground maiden at Ballinaboola; not much to go on but may improve on better ground; trainer and ride combined for Punchestown Grade 1 bumper win last week
runners.comment: Third of six in a heavy-ground maiden at Ballinaboola, not much to go on, ground may suit

runners.name: Coolnav
runners.spotlight: Sholokhov gelding is a brother to hurdle/chase winner Sweet Street, half-brother to two other winners; dam point/bumper winner; failed to make an impact in two bumpers last season; will need to step up on recent placed form in points.
runners.comment: No impact in two bumpers and will need to step up on recent placed form in points

runners.name: Torquemada
runners.spotlight: E24,000 3yo; tenth foal; half-brother by Getaway to useful winners Coolvalla and Cedarwood Road; dam placed in bumper; in good hands with Ellmarie Holden, but failed to shine in two starts in points; blinkered now.
runners.comment: In good hands with Ellmarie Holden but failed to shine in two starts in points blinkered

runners.name: Brootally
runners.spotlight: E22,000 3yo; fifth foal; half-brother to 3m hurdle/3m2f chase winner The Questioner; dam unraced half-sister to the smart Monksland; failed to complete in three starts last season; off for almost a year before justifying favouritism at Loughanmore a month ago; makes long journey for racecourse debut.
runners.comment: Off for almost a year before a much-improved display to win a point, long journey for this

runners.name: Majestic Horizon
runners.spotlight: E45,000 3yo; seventh foal; half-brother by Wings Of Eagles to the ill-fated dual bumper scorer and winning hurdler Dark Raven; dam half-sister to the useful Ted Veale; made a good first impression when winning a maiden point for Cormac Doyle at Rathcannon last November; third and fourth have won on the track.
runners.comment: Won a maiden point for Cormac Doyle last November, third and fourth have won on the track

runners.name: The Farmers Bar
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother by Old Persian to three minor winners Kannapolis; dam, by Hawk Wing, a Flat winner in Italy; ran on towards the finish when third on debut at Dromahane last month; winning favourite Chanson Du Jury was bought by Gordon Elliott for E200,000 at Punchestown last week, runner-up fetched E100,000; stable in form.
runners.comment: Third on debut at Dromahane last month, the winner was an expensive purchase last week

runners.name: Jetaway Mountain
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam French hurdle winner, half-sister to point/bumper winner Love Sign d'Aunou (disappointing in two Grade 1 races since Naas success); went close in a maiden point last October; winner has struggled in three outings over hurdles in Britain.
runners.comment: Went close in a maiden point last October, winner has struggled over hurdles in Britain

runners.name: The Boysofairhill
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to bumper/hurdles winner Long Gone; had shown little before winning a maiden at Dromahane last month on his fifth outing; cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Had shown little before winning a maiden at Dromahane on his fifth outing, cheekpieces on

races.race_name: racingtv.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Fozzie
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on both 2yo starts (7f/6f) but, having been gelded, belied 66-1 odds and showed huge improvement when close second to useful stablemate at Thirsk (7f, good) last month; upped in trip here; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Much improved form when close second to useful stablemate at Thirsk (7f) last month

runners.name: Song Of The Clouds
runners.spotlight: Out of a Listed winner; 15-8, did pretty well to take second in 11-runner newcomers' race at Newbury (7f, good) last month, given she was messed about soon after the start and still looked green; holds a Group 1 entry at Royal Ascot and is evidently held in some regard.
runners.comment: Did well to take second in 7f newcomers' race at Newbury last month; holds Group 1 entry

runners.name: Mehmas Champion
runners.spotlight: Looked promising on all three starts in Germany last spring, most notably when staying-on fourth in their 2,000 Guineas (1m, good; Group 2); changed hands for 14,000gns in the autumn and was gelded; market helpful on stable debut.
runners.comment: Fourth in last year's German 2,000 Guineas; changed hands for 14,000gns in autumn

runners.name: Regal Desire
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to UAE 9.5f dirt winner Withering (RPR 109); dam unraced sister to 5.5f-1m winner Predetermined, half-sister to winners Classic Blade (6f 2yo Group 2), Raadobarg (smart 7f-1m) and Captain Dunne (smart 5f); probably needs to be useful to win on debut.
runners.comment: Out of a well-connected mare but probably needs to be useful to make a winning debut

runners.name: Master Of Entropy
runners.spotlight: Bettered low-key 2yo debut when third on last month's reappearance at Newcastle (1m, AW; first run after wind op); probably needs to take another step forward but has the potential.
runners.comment: Placed on last month's reappearance at Newcastle; needs more but still has potential

runners.name: Go On Ollie
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to 5f AW winner Gleaming Arch (RPR 65); dam fourth in 7f maidens (43), half-sister to two sprint winners; seems very unlikely to meet the standard required on this belated debut.
runners.comment: Seems very unlikely to meet the standard required on this belated debut

runners.name: Sheikhnshah
runners.spotlight: From a good French family and made a promising debut when third at Newcastle (1m, AW; travelled strongly) in November; hooded for this reappearance outing; ought to make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Made a promising debut when third on Tapeta in November; ought to make presence felt

runners.name: It's Debatable
runners.spotlight: Out of a well-connected US winner; recovered from slow start to make a fairly encouraging debut when sixth at Beverley (1m2f, good) a fortnight ago; can improve but others in this field have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Made a fairly encouraging debut last month but others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Jimmy's Army
runners.spotlight: Out of a fair 1m winner; didn't show enough when seventh of ten at Southwell (1m, AW) last month to be of significant interest here.
runners.comment: Didn't show enough on last month's debut to be of significant interest here

runners.name: Clova Royale
runners.spotlight: Always behind when 250-1 for debut at Thirsk (7f) 19 days ago, a race in which Fozzie finished second.
runners.comment: Always behind when 250-1 for recent debut at Thirsk (7f)
runners.owner: Clova Farms And P D Niven

runners.name: Freddie Steady Go
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper in good style last year but very disappointing on hurdle debut in January and made an underwhelming start to his Flat career last month; remains best watched.
runners.comment: Won a bumper last year but made underwhelming start to Flat career 11 days ago
runners.owner: Castle View, A Bell, S Oliver & M Severs

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: One Of Our Own
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; two creditable fourth-place finishes this year; likely to give it another good go under suitable conditions.
runners.comment: C&D winner who has run two solid races in defeat this year; in the mix once again

runners.name: Due Respect
runners.spotlight: Three course runs have yielded a win and two second-place finishes; they came over 7f but she is not short of pace and today's drop in trip could suit; went off too fast on last week's Musselburgh return but she should be sharper here and she's high on the list.
runners.comment: Good course form; sharper for last week's return; appealing claims back at 6f

runners.name: Mister Sky Blue
runners.spotlight: Just over 12 months since he recorded his only success, namely a 6f handicap at Hamilton (good to firm); that win came off a 6lb higher mark and he was second at Newcastle off 2lb higher in November; absent 142 days but he ran well on last season's return; more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but he came to hand early last year and he's on a good mark now; chance

runners.name: Canaria Sun
runners.spotlight: From a family the stable has enjoyed success with and he ran on to take a never-dangerous fourth (of 17) on his Thirsk return 19 days ago; has time to do better but he didn't look overly enthusiastic at Thirsk.
runners.comment: From a successful sprinting family but looked a tricky ride when 4th on his reappearance

runners.name: Auntie Jo
runners.spotlight: Running well since the visor went on, most recently keeping on for fourth at Doncaster 13 days ago (5f, good to firm); each-way shout with a promising 7lb claimer enlisted.
runners.comment: Running well since a visor went on; each-way shout at least with promising claimer booked

runners.name: Indy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Ended her 2025 campaign with a modest effort and she hasn't fired in either run this year; enough to prove for now.
runners.comment: Yet to shine in two runs this year; others bring more compelling claims

runners.name: Ziva's Star
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but the odd run has hinted at better and she wasn't beaten far on her Doncaster return two weeks ago (5f, good to firm); not a solid option but could sneak into the frame.
runners.comment: Exposed 18-race maiden but boasts the odd piece of form that would give her frame claims

runners.name: Let's Go Hugo
runners.spotlight: All wins at 5f but he has run well over C&D; yet to shine in 2026 but dropping into 0-55 company will help; not ruled out.
runners.comment: All wins at 5f but has run well over C&D; drops in grade and can't be ruled out

runners.name: Sadiiki
runners.spotlight: Peak RPR of just 44 in her nine starts; hard to recommend back from a 142-day layoff.
runners.comment: Unplaced all nine starts, recording a peak RPR of 44; hard to recommend

runners.name: She's A Dark Horse
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden who has finished unplaced in all 13 starts; new headgear needs to prove transformative.
runners.comment: Unplaced all 13 starts; new headgear needs to prove transformative

runners.name: Hurstwood
runners.spotlight: Three wins and a second from nine visits to Redcar; looked as though the run would bring him on when in midfield at Thirsk 19 days ago; more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; on fair mark and sharper for recent reappearance; shortlisted
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Night Emperor
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for Hugo Palmer and he finished well beaten (50-1) on his stable debut in February (7f, AW; after 483 days off); down in grade but not easily recommended.
runners.comment: Low-key stable debut in February (7f, AW; after long absence); not easily recommended

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Stormy Pearl
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; had a few of today's rivals behind her when second of 17 at Thirsk 19 days ago, her first outing since November; promising apprentice takes off 7lb; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Promising 2nd at Thirsk on return 19 days ago; good apprentice takes off 7lb; contender

runners.name: Run This Way
runners.spotlight: Won three times on good to soft/soft in 2024, the first of them coming over C&D; not at her best for a while now though and she wouldn't want the ground to dry out too much; first-time blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: C&D winner; on a dangerous mark but quiet for a while & slower ground ideal; new headgear

runners.name: Raja Charger
runners.spotlight: Poor form in four novice runs from 1m to 1m3f; drops in trip with cheekpieces added; risky.
runners.comment: Poor novice form over further; drop in trip/headgear not enough to tempt

runners.name: Starsong
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner who was a close second at Southwell on her return to action in March; unable to match that in two runs since but she has too much ability to discount at this level.
runners.comment: Multiple winner; went close on her return (6f, AW); less good since but can't discount

runners.name: Emerald Army
runners.spotlight: Mixed bag for his former stable but he looked a cut above this level on occasions; joint-favourite for his stable/seasonal debut at Thirsk 19 days ago and ran on for a never-dangerous fifth; he will need to build on that effort to win here but that is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Has looked better than this level on occasions; may come on for stable debut; chance

runners.name: Jamie's Choice
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 5f in the summer of 2024, including at this track; ran well on her seasonal return last April but it has been downhill ever since; 6f a query too.
runners.comment: It's been a while since she was at her best and 6f is the query today

runners.name: A Lady Forever
runners.spotlight: Bits of form that bring her into the reckoning but she hasn't found her best in three AW runs in 2026; she has shown promise in a handful of turf runs but others appeal more for the win.
runners.comment: Not found her best form so far in 2025; return to turf needs to revive her fortunes

runners.name: Barmyblade
runners.spotlight: Three wins on good ground (5f-6f) last spring, the last of them coming off a 4lb higher mark; ended 2025 quietly and yet to find his best in 2026 but there was some encouragement to be gleaned from his latest Catterick fourth; now eligible for 0-55 company and could bounce back to top form.
runners.comment: Well treated on form from last spring; down in grade and offered a bit more at Catterick

runners.name: Jeany May
runners.spotlight: Two uninspiring efforts this year and although she is tumbling down the weights, she has a good deal to prove, including stamina.
runners.comment: Two uninspiring efforts this year and stamina still to be proven; others are much safer

runners.name: Dunnington Lad
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 5f at Ripon in 2024; 2025 was a struggle and, although his mark has bottomed out, he has plenty tot prove on his reappearance.
runners.comment: 2025 was a struggle and he has plenty to prove back from 235 days off

runners.name: Bayraat
runners.spotlight: Getting on for three years since his sole success (6f, good to firm); thrown in on last year's best form and he has run well fresh in the past; not discounted now dropping into a 0-55.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but returns at a low level and he has gone well fresh in the past

races.race_name: Market Cross Jewellers Handicap

runners.name: Tupero
runners.spotlight: 1m4f AW win in February; he has finished second in two of his three runs since, the latest when beaten 10l behind a rival who poached a huge advantage at Ayr on Tuesday (1m7f, good to firm); a personal best will be needed to take this.
runners.comment: Runner-up in both turf runs for D O'Meara, the latest on Tuesday; a career best is needed

runners.name: Kitsune Power
runners.spotlight: It has been nearly two years since the last of his four wins but his two starts in 2026 have seen him finish third; conditions won't be an excuse and he's more solid than some.
runners.comment: Finished 3rd in both starts this year; conditions no problem and should be involved again

runners.name: Bruce Banner
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a small-field handicap at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) last April off a 2lb higher mark, and made a winning debut over hurdles in November; stayed on for third in a 1m4f handicap at Newcastle in March (visored first time) but looked stretched by 2m latest; this trip should be in range and he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Stretched by 2m last time and this trip should suit; one to consider

runners.name: Coverbridge
runners.spotlight: 7f AW win as a 2yo for Philip Makin; generally struggled for current stable though and steps up markedly in distance with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Struggled since joining current stable and steps up markedly in trip with a point to prove

runners.name: Grazeon Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Useful bumper performer; down the field in three Flat starts (1m2f-1m4f) this year but it would be surprising if he didn't prove a different proposition now up in trip for his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Useful in bumpers; yet to shine on the Flat but should be a different proposition today

runners.name: Billy No Mates
runners.spotlight: A veteran who is without a win since September 2023; good weights chance on last season's best but he ended the year with a modest effort; handles good ground but rarely seen on a faster surface.
runners.comment: Winless since September 2023; chance on last year's best but he is returning from a break

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV In Stunning HD Handicap

runners.name: Vince Le Prince
runners.spotlight: Has added to his strong record over 7f at Catterick with a pair of runner-up efforts on good last month; successful on his fast-ground C&D debut, which was his only previous visit to Redcar; should go well again.
runners.comment: Two good seconds at Catterick this spring; won his previous outing over C&D; player

runners.name: Mayo County
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good to firm) off 1lb higher last May and made a positive return to turf when third of 14 to the reopposing Looks Fantastic over C&D (good to firm again) 17 days ago, faring best of three who came towards the stands' side; has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: C&D win last May and third to Looks Fantastic here last month; enters calculations

runners.name: Lord Capulet
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who landed his fourth 7f Newcastle success of the year when seeing off 13 rivals last month; his 2lb nudge for that is offset by his rider's claim; 0-4 on turf but probably a bit premature to suggest he won't prove fully effective on the grass.
runners.comment: Improver on AW this year, winning four times; claims if he can translate it to turf

runners.name: Tropez Power
runners.spotlight: Gained sole turf success in 2021 and it's two years since last AW win; 0-5 for current stable but he could strip fitter for his Beverley reappearance a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Sole turf success back in 2021 and two years since latest AW win; sixth on reappearance

runners.name: Yafaarr
runners.spotlight: In decent nick on AW for this yard in the second half of 2025 but hasn't been able to translate that to turf on return this spring; being eased 2lb can only help but others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: In decent nick on AW in second half of 2025 but not in quite same form on turf this spring

runners.name: Looks Fantastic
runners.spotlight: Built on an encouraging AW reappearance when gaining a breakthrough win in 14-runner C&D handicap (good to firm; Mayo County third) 17 days ago; they were quite well strung out in behind (over 4l back to the fourth) so a 3lb rise looks manageable.
runners.comment: 3lb rise for recent C&D wins looks fair given they were quite well strung out in behind

runners.name: The New Bay Pearl
runners.spotlight: 0-8 for Roger Varian and well held on 7f Doncaster reappearance for new yard 13 days ago; can only watch.
runners.comment: 0-8 for Roger Varian and well held on recent return for new stable

runners.name: Singoura
runners.spotlight: AW winner as a juvenile but has drawn a blank in eight handicaps since; latest fourth of 16 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) was creditable but underlines she needs a bit more from this mark.
runners.comment: Latest Doncaster fourth was creditable but took handicap record to 0-8

runners.name: Front Gunner
runners.spotlight: Possibly stretched by 1m when sixth of nine back on turf at Thirsk last weekend (final start for Stephen Hanlon); new stable is quick to fit him with a tongue-tie and send him back to scene of his only turf win (on good to firm last summer); due to be 2lb lower from the weekend but still worth a betting check.
runners.comment: Faded over 1m on Saturday; changed yard since and new yard fit tongue-tie; C&D winner

runners.name: Palmarian
runners.spotlight: Capable of fair form but he failed to go on for John and Thady Gosden last season and didn't offer obvious promise in two outings for this yard last month.
runners.comment: No obvious promise in two runs for new yard last month
runners.owner: MDP Racing And Mrs R Carr

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Two B Tanned
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won five times last April-July (good/good to firm) and took it up another notch with easy all-the-way win in 13-runner Musselburgh handicap (1m, good) under Tom Kiely-Marshall on reappearance 11 days ago; that was an apprentice race so she escapes a penalty and is 9lb well in compared to her future mark.
runners.comment: Bolted up on 1m Musselburgh reappearance and 9lb well in compared to future mark

runners.name: Rainbow Nebula
runners.spotlight: Made steady progress switched to handicaps last autumn, winning over 7f at Ascot (good to soft) and 1m at Newcastle (AW); that makes his reappearance effort in the Doncaster Spring Mile disappointing but last year's exploits show he's better than that.
runners.comment: Disappointed on reappearance but made steady progress late last year; could bounce back

runners.name: Double Parked
runners.spotlight: This C&D brought out the best in him last season, with two wins in the summer (good to soft/good to firm) and a close third of 13 in the autumn; faded as if needing his reappearance back here 17 days ago and ought to be sharper now.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner last summer and likely needed last month's reappearance here

runners.name: Mr Mistoffelees
runners.spotlight: In good form in blinkers towards the end of last year, winning at Newcastle (1m, AW) in October; however, he has a poor strike-rate and wasn't at his best in cheekpieces at Newcastle (1m, AW) six weeks ago following a break; cheekpieces remain on today.
runners.comment: Has poor 2-30 strike-rate and not at his best on reappearance; others more obvious

runners.name: Nyman
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo whose came good at the second attempt for Roger Fell in 7f Newmarket handicap (good to soft) last backend; has returned with respectable efforts on AW and turf in recent months and now only 1lb above the mark he defied at Newmarket; cheekpieces are refitted for the first time in Britain.
runners.comment: Won at Newmarket last autumn and two respectable comeback runs this spring; hood refitted

runners.name: El Matador
runners.spotlight: This low-mileage 4yo found the line coming a few strides too soon when odds-on favourite for his 7f Lingfield (AW) reappearance ten days ago; that points to this return to 1m suiting one who is back out a couple of days before a 3lb rise for Lingfield takes effect; his turf wins last year came on good to firm and good; should go well.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied on 7f AW return last week and out again before 3lb rise kicks in

runners.name: Real Gain
runners.spotlight: Very useful for Richard Hughes, including third in the Golden Mile at Goodwood (1m, soft) on last year's reappearance; failed to back that up in two subsequent outings in 2025 but added to his solid record fresh when fourth on 1m Newmarket return for new yard 23 days ago; classy but needs to show he can still string good efforts together now.
runners.comment: Added to solid record fresh when fourth at Newmarket for new yard; needs to back it up

runners.name: Mr King
runners.spotlight: Successful twice (1m2f/1m) in Ireland earlier in his career; talented but failed to win in 14 starts for Iain Jardine, including refusing to race on his penultimate outing last year; it's possible a change of stable (same owner) could spark a revival but a watching brief is still the percentage call here; hooded first time.
runners.comment: Talented but went 0-14 for Iain Jardine, including refusing to race; new yard tries a hood

runners.name: Ron O
runners.spotlight: Developed into a very useful handicapper for Ray Craggs in 2024, including a C&D win on good to firm; failed to scale the same heights for David Thompson in 2025 but has a reduced mark to work with now back under the care of his owner.
runners.comment: Very useful at his best and reduced mark to work with now back with his owner-trainer

races.race_name: Marske Fabrication & Engineering Handicap (Marske Fabrication & Engineeriing Straight Mile Qual')

runners.name: Mohmentous
runners.spotlight: Much improved tackling turf for the first time when drawing nearly 4l clear in 1m Bath handicap (firm) last Wednesday; well treated under a 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Came clear in 1m Bath handicap last week and well treated under a penalty

runners.name: Very Demure
runners.spotlight: No impact on AW on first five starts but better signs switched to turf last month, finishing 1l second of nine at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) in first-time cheekpieces (retained); enters calculations off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: First turf start when second over 1m at Yarmouth in cheekpieces latest; claims

runners.name: Baldosa
runners.spotlight: Enters calculations on her solid 6f Newcastle third in March but well held over 6f/7f on turf since; it remains to be seen whether the step up to 1m makes a difference.
runners.comment: Good third on AW in March but well held over 6f and 7f on turf since

runners.name: Harswell River
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly at Wetherby only 11 days ago but her two best RPRs have come over this C&D, namely a win on good to soft last year and a third on good to firm last month.
runners.comment: Well held at Wetherby latest but two C&D outings have yielded a win and third

runners.name: Battenburg Belle
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for close third of 14 on handicap debut at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) last month but never involved when stepped up to 1m here since.
runners.comment: Third on 6f handicap debut last month but need to forgive lesser run over C&D since

runners.name: Lope Y Linda
runners.spotlight: Poor form, although she did make some late gains for fifth on last week's C&D reappearance (good to firm) and may come on for the outing.
runners.comment: Poor form but is entitled to come on for last week's C&D reappearance fifth

runners.name: Leysthorpe Star
runners.spotlight: Yet to achieve much of worth, including always in rear on 1m Ripon handicap debut three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Little form of worth, including well held on handicap debut

runners.name: Muvable
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three runs last term and fared no better for a switch to handicaps with cheekpieces added when last of seven on 6f course reappearance (good to firm) 17 days ago; it remains to be seen whether a 2f longer trip makes any difference.
runners.comment: Well held on 6f course reappearance and remains to be seen whether longer trip helps

runners.name: Give Me Sun
runners.spotlight: Cut little ice in three 7f maidens at 2yrs and similar story in 7f Leicester handicap on reappearance; early days but hard to make a case for based on what she's done so far.
runners.comment: Well held on 7f handicap debut/reappearance; can only watch after that
runners.owner: Mrs Denis Haynes & Mrs Rebecca Smith

runners.name: A Boy Named Mary
runners.spotlight: Failed to make an impact last year, including last of seven on 6f Newcastle nursery debut in December; steps back up in trip on return; improvement will be needed.
runners.comment: Failed to make an impact last year, including a 6f nursery; improvement needed

runners.name: King Of Yorkshire
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three 7f novice runs last autumn but the fact he's a half-brother to useful winners in France and the US marks him down as a potential improver in low-level handicaps; one to keep an eye on in the betting.
runners.comment: Well held in three novices last autumn but possible improver in handicaps; check betting

races.race_name: Watch Race Replays At racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: Woody Y Fernandez
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times for Jennie Candlish, including on turf, and again when third of eight on 6f Wolverhampton stable debut 37 days ago; ought to be competitive.
runners.comment: Placed on five of 11 starts, including on AW stable debut; ought to be competitive

runners.name: Auspicious
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on his nursery debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) last September; creditable third there (6f again) in March but only fifth (Long Shot 5l ahead in third) back on turf here 17 days ago.
runners.comment: One of only two winners in the line-up but only a well-held fifth over C&D latest

runners.name: Dream Forever
runners.spotlight: Reached the frame in 6f/7f handicaps at Newcastle this year; beaten a long way on all three outings on turf, although the outside stall provides an excuse for his heavy defeat in a big-field handicap at Pontefract last month.
runners.comment: Reached frame in AW handicaps first two runs this year and poor draw back on turf since

runners.name: Ganthorpe
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but his 1l third of eight at Wolverhampton (6f) in February shows he can be competitive from a basement mark; also a respectable fifth of 15 over 6f at Nottingham (good) 11 days ago; unlikely to be far away.
runners.comment: 0-8 but has shown a few times he can be very competitive from a low mark, including latest

runners.name: Miss Magic Dragon
runners.spotlight: Improved at the second time of asking in handicaps when shading a tight finish over the extended 5f at Bath (good) on Monday; not obviously well treated under a 6lb penalty turned out again quickly but the fact she's a winner counts for plenty at this level.
runners.comment: Shaded a tight finish at Bath on Monday and claims under penalty turned out again quickly

runners.name: Brain Freeze
runners.spotlight: No improvement for the switch to nurseries on turf last autumn and weakened to finish well held on AW reappearance 24 days ago; others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Well held all three handicaps, including reappearance

runners.name: Monk's Hill
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but mainly over 7f/1m and she shaped as if the drop back to 6f may suit when fourth of 12 over 7f at Wolverhampton ten days ago, leading until inside the final furlong; also equipped with a first-time tongue-tie; gets the vote.
runners.comment: Led for long way over 7f on AW last week so return to 6f could be good move; tongue-tie on
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: The Floors Munky
runners.spotlight: Well held on all three starts (trained by Iain Jardine on debut) but low-grade handicaps provide him with a more realistic chance.
runners.comment: Well held on all three starts but has more realistic chance in handicaps

runners.name: I'm Dan Dare
runners.spotlight: Moderate form in three qualifying runs at the end of last year and well held on 5f Nottingham handicap debut and reappearance 19 days ago; first-time cheekpieces need to spark improvement.
runners.comment: Well held on 5f Nottingham handicap debut/return; cheekpieces need to spark improvement

runners.name: Long Shot
runners.spotlight: Best effort when third of eight on C&D reappearance (good to firm) with cheekpieces added (retained) 17 days ago; entitled to come on for the outing and one of the more likely types.
runners.comment: Good third on C&D reappearance and entitled to come on for the outing; player

runners.name: Noble Gentleman
runners.spotlight: Off the bridle in rear from an early stage but did make some late headway when 4l eighth of 15 on 6f Nottingham handicap debut 11 days ago, with the reopposing Ganthorpe just over 1l ahead in fifth; improvement possible if today's first-time cheekpieces help him to travel with more fluency.
runners.comment: Late headway for eighth of 15 on handicap debut; cheekpieces may sharpen him up

runners.name: Charcon
runners.spotlight: Only poor form shown to date, including two AW handicaps this winter; a first-time visor needs to spark improvement back on turf after 12 weeks off.
runners.comment: Poor form so far and first-time visor needs to spark improvement back on turf

runners.name: Kwidsin
runners.spotlight: Has yet to build on last summer's 7f Musselburgh debut but it's possible his 7f Southwell handicap debut and reappearance six weeks ago was needed; reverts to sprinting now.
runners.comment: Yet to build on debut, although he might have needed outing on handicap debut/return

runners.name: Hares Bredth
runners.spotlight: Five-raced maiden who was a well-held fourth on his 6f Ripon reappearance 12 days ago; may strip fitter but Long Shot is the more obvious of the Tim Easterby pair.
runners.comment: Well-held fourth on 6f Ripon reappearance and Long Shot more obvious of yard's pair

runners.name: Powernap

runners.comment: Won very comfortably over C&D in March and again ran well at Southwell last month

runners.name: Ice In The Veins
runners.spotlight: 2-7 over hurdles last season; raced too freely and disappointed when favourite for a valuable series final last month but makes chasing debut from a good mark; big player if he gets into a good jumping rhythm.
runners.comment: 2-7 over hurdles last season; makes chasing debut from a good mark; interesting

runners.name: Pep Talking
runners.spotlight: Took form to another new level when winning by 10l from the front at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) last month and is now 7-17 over fences (includes course win); today's trip-and-ground combination will also suit; respected.
runners.comment: Career-best form when winning by 10l at Chepstow last month; now 7-17 over fences

runners.name: William Cody
runners.spotlight: Gained three of four chase wins over C&D and returns here after going close on final two starts last season (2m4f/2m3f; wore cheekpieces last time); holds no secrets from the handicapper but will be fine on the forecast good ground and has obvious claims.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; returns here after going close on last two outings; obvious claims

runners.name: Koapey
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo; three non-completions have followed last October's promising chase/handicap debut but he was running fairly well before penultimate-fence fall in 2m Chepstow race won by Pep Talking last month; moves up in trip with Sean Bowen enlisted here; still has plenty of time to get career back on track.
runners.comment: Three non-completions have followed his promising chase debut; can still get back on track

runners.name: Hurricane Bay
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped if judged on his two good-ground wins in cheekpieces last spring (2m7f/2m5f) but has been well below best (in various headgear) in recent months; ran well for a long way before weakening quickly at Market Rasen (3m, good) last time and might benefit from this drop back in trip but others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way over 3m last time but has been well below his best for a while now

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Caturra Lights
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a 6f winner; belied his 66-1 odds with close third of eight, faring best of the newcomers, in Musselburgh maiden (5f, good); sets the standard, assuming he can back up that form.
runners.comment: Sets the standard, assuming the form of his 66-1 third at Musselburgh is backed up

runners.name: Jazz Queen
runners.spotlight: Related to a Nunthorpe winner; perhaps hindered by ending up rather isolated towards the near side in maiden (5f, good) at Newmarket, finishing sixth of eight; this local runner is likely to do better, provided she can overcome wide draw.
runners.comment: Perhaps hindered ending up rather isolated in Newmarket maiden; likely to do better

runners.name: Koodini
runners.spotlight: March foal; 20,000euros yearling; sibling to two winners, notably Old Chums (6f; RPR 84); dam 6f/1m scorer (including 2yo/Listed; 103), half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Piece Of Paradise; suitably bred, nicely drawn and represents a locally based trainer who landed this race with a debutant last year; interesting.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling; suitably bred, nicely drawn and looks an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Donegal Rose
runners.spotlight: Closely related to a 7f AW 2yo winner; the form of her fifth of 12 at Leicester (5f, good to firm) needs marking up a tad as she was hampered in the closing stages; local runner who has Oisin Murphy on board and warrants respect with improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: Form of her Leicester effort needs marking up as she was hampered in the closing stages

runners.name: Andalnade
runners.spotlight: April foal; 52,000euros foal, 37,000gns yearling; Showcasing half-sister to five winners, most notably Sacramento (Australian 9.5f/1m2f; RPR 97); dam 7f AW 2yo scorer (88); wears tongue-tie on debut; has the plum draw.
runners.comment: 37,000gns yearling; by Showcasing; wears tongue-tie on debut; has the plum draw

runners.name: Penny Capri
runners.spotlight: January foal; 32,000euros foal, 42,000gns yearling, £50,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Minzaal and first foal of 6.5f-1m winner (RPR 94); fitting of hood on debut suggests she's possibly fizzy; market may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: £50,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Minzaal; wears hood on debut; check the betting

runners.name: Paper View
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to seven winners; unable to justify favouritism but showed some promise with fourth of ten at Bath (5f, good) and ought to progress; possibilities, provided he's able to overcome the widest draw.
runners.comment: May build on Bath promise, provided he's able to overcome the widest draw

runners.name: Dubai Champion
runners.spotlight: March foal; 38,000euros foal, 40,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Persian Force half-brother to two winners, notably Mansa Musa (5.5f/6f including 2yo/US Listed; RPR 113); dam 6f AW scorer (74); well-drawn debutant.
runners.comment: 40,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Persian Force; well-drawn debutant
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Sunrise At Dawn
runners.spotlight: Made encouraging late gains on her way to fifth of 11 at Beverley (5f, good to soft) and may improve with the experience under her belt, albeit is perhaps going to need a strong pace if again closing from rear.
runners.comment: Made late gains at Beverley but may need a strong pace if again closing from rear

runners.name: Undercover Affair
runners.spotlight: Speedily bred gelding who finished about 2l fourth of seven in Bath maiden (5.7f, firm) won by a 40-1 stablemate, shaping as if this sharper scenario will suit; in the mix, provided he can surmount wide draw.
runners.comment: Speedily bred; Bath effort suggests this sharper scenario will suit; in the mix

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced at Beverley (5f) on Monday but it's questionable whether he improved on his effort behind Caturra Lights at Musselburgh; vulnerable sort, taking on once-raced rivals and newcomers.
runners.comment: Questionable whether he improved on Monday; vulnerable in this field

races.race_name: Boodles Raindance Dee Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Morshdi
runners.spotlight: Dante and Derby entry; brother to 1m2f and 14.5f (Group 2) winner Nakheel (RPR 110); won at York (1m, good) on his second start as 2yo and followed up in clearcut style in a seven-runner Listed race at Newmarket (1m1f, good) three weeks ago; has one of the two best chances on form and he's on his way up.
runners.comment: Won second 2yo start and in clearcut style in 1m1f Listed race at Newmarket last month

runners.name: Constitution River
runners.spotlight: Group 1 entry at 1m, 1m2f and 1m4f; by Wootton Bassett out of unplaced sister to 1m4f/1m6f Group 1 winner Wonderful Tonight; short-headed at Newmarket before making all when odds-in in both a Galway maiden (7f, yielding) last July and five-runner Group 2 at the Curragh (7f, good) last August; both wins came with authority and the pedigree is encouraging for 1m2f; his enthusiasm for getting on with it last year raises more of a question but the 1m2f French Derby has been mentioned by his trainer as an objective.
runners.comment: Enthusiasm for getting on with it and twice made all over 7f as 2yo, notably in a Group 2
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Generic
runners.spotlight: By Kameko out of 1m2f winner (77) with Group performers in family; led over 1f out when pipped in a Kempton maiden (1m, AW) in March but a win never seemed in doubt when he made virtually all in Yarmouth novice (1m, good) 17 days later; looks promising for 1m2f but this race is much more demanding.
runners.comment: Looks promising for 1m2f but this is much more demanding than the Yarmouth novice he won

runners.name: Flushing Meadows
runners.spotlight: Group 1 entry at 1m, 1m2f and 1m4f, as is his stablemate Constitution River (also by Wootton Bassett); can't match his form, having won on debut but come up short in three Group 3s over 6.5f to just over 7f; joint favourite at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance but slowly away and never showed with any chance; it's possible that 1m2f will see him in a different light but Constitution River looks the stable first string.
runners.comment: 1m2f might see him in a different light but Constitution River looks the yard first string
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Shayem
runners.spotlight: Dante entry; progressive with 3-4 record in Britain last season, ending with a Listed win from the front at Pontefract (1m, soft); however, beaten a long way in Saudi Arabia this February and although back near his 2yo form in Listed race at Newmarket (1m1f, good; disputed lead 2f out) three weeks ago, that was good enough for only fifth of seven to Morshdi; major work to do.
runners.comment: 3-4 record in Britain last season; only fifth of seven to Morshdi in a recent Listed race

runners.name: Golden Story
runners.spotlight: Dante and Derby entry; by Lope De Vega out of well-bred mare whose three earlier offspring have best RPRs of 112, 103 and 112, including this one's brother the 2025 Great Voltigeur third Arabian Force; 13-8 favourite when winning a five-runner maiden at Doncaster (1m, soft) last October, leading 2f out and looking set for a wide-margin win before rather pulling himself up in the last half furlong; probably best forgiven that indiscretion first time out, in which case he's a good prospect for 1m2f+.
runners.comment: Rather pulled himself up when winning at Doncaster (1m, soft); otherwise a good prospect

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Ormonde Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Jan Brueghel
runners.spotlight: Underperformed in the King George on last appearance, having set a pace that perhaps wasn't strong enough given his proven stamina (St Leger winner); cheekpieces worn that day are now removed; mostly progressive otherwise, still has a good strike-rate (5-7) and showed tip-top form to beat Calandagan in the 2025 Coronation Cup at Epsom; the pick on ratings, accordingly.
runners.comment: Record is 5-7; showed tip-top form to win the 2025 Coronation Cup; the pick on ratings
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Mount Atlas
runners.spotlight: Close second in Listed events at Chester (12.5f) last September and Musselburgh (1m6f) on reappearance, latter behind Al Qareem who gave 5lb and is now better off; further behind that opponent when fifth of six in this race last year; needs a personal best.
runners.comment: Ran well on reappearance but this is harder; fifth of six in this contest last year

runners.name: Rahiebb
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive in his first season last year, despite being 0-6 after debut success; neck second to market leader and emerging star stayer Scandinavia in the St Leger (14.5f, soft) final start; threatening to land a major prize and, the way his figures are going, this 4yo season could prove fruitful; an interesting challenger to the two Ballydoyle runners in this field.
runners.comment: Neck second in the St Leger; looks an interesting challenger to the Ballydoyle runners

runners.name: Beylerbeyi
runners.spotlight: Versatile and consistent handicapper who landed the 2025 Cesarewitch (2m2f) in most recent turf attempt and has been placed in all AW starts (1m2f-2m) this year; however, faces a very difficult task at the weights upped in class.
runners.comment: Versatile and consistent handicapper but faces a difficult task upped in class

runners.name: Sons And Lovers
runners.spotlight: Very useful for current yard; has been on his travels this year, winning 1m7f handicap at Riyadh then creditable fourth over 2m at Meydan, both Group 2; however, incurs 5lb penalty and faces a stiffer assignment conceding weight all round in this field.
runners.comment: Incurs 5lb penalty for Group 2 handicap win at Riyadh; faces a stiffer assignment

runners.name: Illinois
runners.spotlight: Ran as if something was amiss in the Irish St Leger on last appearance; very consistent otherwise and justified favouritism in this contest (good) 12 months ago, prior to second-place finishes in two Cup races; beaten only a neck by stablemate Jan Brueghel in the 2024 St Leger; respected in rematch with that rival.
runners.comment: Beaten only a neck by stablemate Jan Brueghel in 2024 St Leger; won this race last year
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Al Qareem
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism in Listed grade at Musselburgh (1m6f, soft) on seasonal debut, making it five wins from his last nine starts and taking overall record to 13-36; Chester record reads 112, most recently running creditably in this race 12 months ago; again likely to give his running.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate; Chester record reads 112, the final effort in this race 12 months ago

runners.name: Real Dream
runners.spotlight: Best effort this year, but still below peak form, when close fourth to Sons And Lovers in Group 2 handicap at Riyadh; failed to beat a rival on AW last time, hasn't won since 2023 and faces a much stiffer assignment on these terms.
runners.comment: Hasn't won since 2023 and faces a stiff task on these terms

races.race_name: Xenon Workplace Handicap

runners.name: Factual
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt whose solid novice record comprises a good effort flanked by turf/AW wins, latest over 7f at Newcastle 11 weeks ago when showing a game attitude by rallying; looks the type who could progress and do well in handicaps; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Has a solid novice record; looks the type who could progress and do well in handicaps

runners.name: Strength Of Spirit
runners.spotlight: Winner of Chester seller (7f) last June and has shown progress to add two handicaps elsewhere (overall record is 3-6); good third at Haydock (1m, good to firm) most recently; holds solid claims and should go well.
runners.comment: Record of 3-6 includes a Chester win; good effort last time; solid and should go well
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Cool Molly
runners.spotlight: Progressive on AW; has a record of 4-6 this year and 2-2 since upped to 7f, most recently scoring at Southwell; the question is whether she can transfer the excellent work to this new scenario (turf debut).
runners.comment: Progressive on AW; record of 4-6 this year; switch to turf is the question mark

runners.name: Utmost Good Faith
runners.spotlight: Turf winner (claimer) in Ireland but has shown better RPRs on AW for new stable; failed to transfer the form back to this sphere latest start, finishing last of six at Musselburgh; others preferred.
runners.comment: Failed to transfer his AW form back to turf last time; others preferred

runners.name: Moonfall
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since finishing last of seven in Newmarket Group 2 last July; solid novice form otherwise, culminating in Newbury success from the front (6.5f, good); may still have more to offer and looks interesting dropped back in class.
runners.comment: Solid novice form prior to Group 2 defeat; may still have more to offer; interesting
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Archer Royal
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant whose chance off top weight partly depends on whether he can reproduce peak effort, namely third in 7f Group 3 at Newmarket last September, which was the second of his four outings.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant whose chance depends on whether he can reproduce peak effort (Group 3)

runners.name: McMurray
runners.spotlight: Has a record of 3-5 overall and 2-2 since handicapping, most recently beating sole rival at Yarmouth (1m, good) seven months ago; beat a total of 16 opponents in gaining his other wins; major contender, with further improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Has a record of 3-5 overall and 2-2 since handicapping; further improvement is plausible
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: I'm Just Ken
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding whose novice form suggests he could do well in handicaps at the right level; however, this is a tough starting point and he's the only maiden in the field; yard also runs King Of Thunder.
runners.comment: Could do well in handicaps at the right level but this is a tough starting point

runners.name: Command The Stars
runners.spotlight: Has seemed a non-stayer over 1m2f and 1m1f (did too much up front) in last two appearances; steadily progressive form at 7f-1m otherwise, culminating in French Listed win; hood now fitted; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Drops back from 1m1f; has steadily progressive form in his 7f-1m races; not ruled out
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Monarch's Gold
runners.spotlight: Campaigned only at 6f, mostly on AW (admittedly went close in sole turf attempt); soundly beaten on finals day at Newcastle most recently; 0-3 since handicapping and now goes into unknown territory trip-wise.
runners.comment: Campaigned only over 6f, mostly on AW; goes into unknown territory trip-wise

runners.name: King Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Local runner who is 1-1 at handicap level and may well show further progress; gelded prior to that Haydock success (7f, good to soft; held on narrowly) in October; should be well primed for this reappearance assignment, given the location, and warrants major respect.
runners.comment: Local runner bidding to make it 2-2 in handicaps; should be well primed; highly respected

runners.name: Caballo Grande
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-2 on AW; doesn't look particularly solid on his two turf efforts, latest at Newbury (handicap debut) where he ended up last of 14 having raced freely and been eased once dropping away.
runners.comment: Record of 2-2 on AW; ended up last of 14 at Newbury returned to turf latest start

runners.name: Aqua Bear
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish runner who has been absent and gelded since justifying favouritism in Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) in November on stable debut; needs progress to follow up but market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Gelded since winning AW maiden in November on stable debut; needs progress to follow up

runners.name: Gentle George
runners.spotlight: Showed progressive RPRs over 5f/6f for Mick Appleby, winning twice prior to an apparently good effort on Champions Day (mark raised 6lb for that); sold for 90,000gns since; something to prove upped in trip.
runners.comment: Showed his 2yo form for Mick Appleby over 5f/6f; something to prove upped in trip

races.race_name: Boodles Raindance Dee Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)
